// age / plugin/plugin.go:29-118
29  type Plugin struct {
30  	name string
31  	fs   *flag.FlagSet
32  	sm   *string
33  
34  	recipient     func([]byte) (age.Recipient, error)
35  	idAsRecipient func([]byte) (age.Recipient, error)
36  	identity      func([]byte) (age.Identity, error)
37  
38  	stdin          io.Reader
39  	stdout, stderr io.Writer
40  
41  	sr *format.StanzaReader
42  	// broken is set if the protocol broke down during an interaction function
43  	// called by a Recipient or Identity.
44  	broken bool
45  }
46  
47  // New creates a new Plugin with the given name.
48  //
49  // For example, a plugin named "frood" would be invoked as "age-plugin-frood".
50  func New(name string) (*Plugin, error) {
51  	return &Plugin{name: name, stdin: os.Stdin,
52  		stdout: os.Stdout, stderr: os.Stderr}, nil
53  }
54  
55  // Name returns the name of the plugin.
56  func (p *Plugin) Name() string {
57  	return p.name
58  }
59  
60  // RegisterFlags registers the plugin's flags with the given [flag.FlagSet], or
61  // with the default [flag.CommandLine] if fs is nil. It must be called before
62  // [flag.Parse] and [Plugin.Main].
63  //
64  // This allows the plugin to expose additional flags when invoked manually, for
65  // example to implement a keygen mode.
66  func (p *Plugin) RegisterFlags(fs *flag.FlagSet) {
67  	if fs == nil {
68  		fs = flag.CommandLine
69  	}
70  	p.fs = fs
71  	p.sm = fs.String("age-plugin", "", "age-plugin state machine")
72  }
73  
74  // HandleRecipient registers a function to parse recipients of the form
75  // age1name1... into [age.Recipient] values. data is the decoded Bech32 payload.
76  //
77  // If the returned Recipient implements [age.RecipientWithLabels], Plugin will
78  // use it and enforce consistency across every returned stanza in an execution.
79  // If the client supports labels, they will be passed through the protocol.
80  //
81  // It must be called before [Plugin.Main], and can be called at most once.
82  // Otherwise, it panics.
83  func (p *Plugin) HandleRecipient(f func(data []byte) (age.Recipient, error)) {
84  	if p.recipient != nil {
85  		panic("HandleRecipient called twice")
86  	}
87  	p.recipient = f
88  }
89  
90  // HandleIdentityAsRecipient registers a function to parse identities of the
91  // form AGE-PLUGIN-NAME-1... into [age.Recipient] values, for when identities
92  // are used as recipients. data is the decoded Bech32 payload.
93  //
94  // If the returned Recipient implements [age.RecipientWithLabels], Plugin will
95  // use it and enforce consistency across every returned stanza in an execution.
96  // If the client supports labels, they will be passed through the protocol.
97  //
98  // It must be called before [Plugin.Main], and can be called at most once.
99  // Otherwise, it panics.
100  func (p *Plugin) HandleIdentityAsRecipient(f func(data []byte) (age.Recipient, error)) {
101  	if p.idAsRecipient != nil {
102  		panic("HandleIdentityAsRecipient called twice")
103  	}
104  	p.idAsRecipient = f
105  }
106  
107  // HandleIdentity registers a function to parse identities of the form
108  // AGE-PLUGIN-NAME-1... into [age.Identity] values. data is the decoded Bech32
109  // payload.
110  //
111  // It must be called before [Plugin.Main], and can be called at most once.
112  // Otherwise, it panics.
113  func (p *Plugin) HandleIdentity(f func(data []byte) (age.Identity, error)) {
114  	if p.identity != nil {
115  		panic("HandleIdentity called twice")
116  	}
117  	p.identity = f
118  }

// age / cmd/age/age.go:105-321
105  func main() {
106  	flag.Usage = func() { fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "%s\n", usage) }
107  
108  	if len(os.Args) == 1 {
109  		flag.Usage()
110  		os.Exit(1)
111  	}
112  
113  	var (
114  		outFlag                          string
115  		decryptFlag, encryptFlag         bool
116  		passFlag, versionFlag, armorFlag bool
117  		recipientFlags                   multiFlag
118  		recipientsFileFlags              multiFlag
119  		identityFlags                    identityFlags
120  	)
121  
122  	flag.BoolVar(&versionFlag, "version", false, "print the version")
123  	flag.BoolVar(&decryptFlag, "d", false, "decrypt the input")
124  	flag.BoolVar(&decryptFlag, "decrypt", false, "decrypt the input")
125  	flag.BoolVar(&encryptFlag, "e", false, "encrypt the input")
126  	flag.BoolVar(&encryptFlag, "encrypt", false, "encrypt the input")
127  	flag.BoolVar(&passFlag, "p", false, "use a passphrase")
128  	flag.BoolVar(&passFlag, "passphrase", false, "use a passphrase")
129  	flag.StringVar(&outFlag, "o", "", "output to `FILE` (default stdout)")
130  	flag.StringVar(&outFlag, "output", "", "output to `FILE` (default stdout)")
131  	flag.BoolVar(&armorFlag, "a", false, "generate an armored file")
132  	flag.BoolVar(&armorFlag, "armor", false, "generate an armored file")
133  	flag.Var(&recipientFlags, "r", "recipient (can be repeated)")
134  	flag.Var(&recipientFlags, "recipient", "recipient (can be repeated)")
135  	flag.Var(&recipientsFileFlags, "R", "recipients file (can be repeated)")
136  	flag.Var(&recipientsFileFlags, "recipients-file", "recipients file (can be repeated)")
137  	flag.Func("i", "identity (can be repeated)", identityFlags.addIdentityFlag)
138  	flag.Func("identity", "identity (can be repeated)", identityFlags.addIdentityFlag)
139  	flag.Func("j", "data-less plugin (can be repeated)", identityFlags.addPluginFlag)
140  	flag.Parse()
141  
142  	if versionFlag {
143  		if buildInfo, ok := debug.ReadBuildInfo(); ok && Version == "" {
144  			Version = buildInfo.Main.Version
145  		}
146  		fmt.Println(Version)
147  		return
148  	}
149  
150  	if flag.NArg() > 1 {
151  		var hints []string
152  		quotedArgs := strings.Trim(fmt.Sprintf("%q", flag.Args()), "[]")
153  
154  		// If the second argument looks like a flag, suggest moving the first
155  		// argument to the back (as long as the arguments don't need quoting).
156  		if strings.HasPrefix(flag.Arg(1), "-") {
157  			hints = append(hints, "the input file must be specified after all flags")
158  
159  			safe := true
160  			unsafeShell := regexp.MustCompile(`[^\w@%+=:,./-]`)
161  			if slices.ContainsFunc(os.Args, unsafeShell.MatchString) {
162  				safe = false
163  			}
164  			if safe {
165  				i := len(os.Args) - flag.NArg()
166  				newArgs := append([]string{}, os.Args[:i]...)
167  				newArgs = append(newArgs, os.Args[i+1:]...)
168  				newArgs = append(newArgs, os.Args[i])
169  				hints = append(hints, "did you mean:")
170  				hints = append(hints, "    "+strings.Join(newArgs, " "))
171  			}
172  		} else {
173  			hints = append(hints, "only a single input file may be specified at a time")
174  		}
175  
176  		errorWithHint("too many INPUT arguments: "+quotedArgs, hints...)
177  	}
178  
179  	switch {
180  	case decryptFlag:
181  		if encryptFlag {
182  			errorf("-e/--encrypt can't be used with -d/--decrypt")
183  		}
184  		if armorFlag {
185  			errorWithHint("-a/--armor can't be used with -d/--decrypt",
186  				"note that armored files are detected automatically, try again without -a/--armor")
187  		}
188  		if passFlag {
189  			errorWithHint("-p/--passphrase can't be used with -d/--decrypt",
190  				"note that password protected files are detected automatically")
191  		}
192  		if len(recipientFlags) > 0 {
193  			errorWithHint("-r/--recipient can't be used with -d/--decrypt",
194  				"did you mean to use -i/--identity to specify a private key?")
195  		}
196  		if len(recipientsFileFlags) > 0 {
197  			errorWithHint("-R/--recipients-file can't be used with -d/--decrypt",
198  				"did you mean to use -i/--identity to specify a private key?")
199  		}
200  	default: // encrypt
201  		if len(identityFlags) > 0 && !encryptFlag {
202  			errorWithHint("-i/--identity and -j can't be used in encryption mode unless symmetric encryption is explicitly selected with -e/--encrypt",
203  				"did you forget to specify -d/--decrypt?")
204  		}
205  		if len(recipientFlags)+len(recipientsFileFlags)+len(identityFlags) == 0 && !passFlag {
206  			errorWithHint("missing recipients",
207  				"did you forget to specify -r/--recipient, -R/--recipients-file or -p/--passphrase?")
208  		}
209  		if len(recipientFlags) > 0 && passFlag {
210  			errorf("-p/--passphrase can't be combined with -r/--recipient")
211  		}
212  		if len(recipientsFileFlags) > 0 && passFlag {
213  			errorf("-p/--passphrase can't be combined with -R/--recipients-file")
214  		}
215  		if len(identityFlags) > 0 && passFlag {
216  			errorf("-p/--passphrase can't be combined with -i/--identity and -j")
217  		}
218  	}
219  
220  	warnDuplicates(slices.Values(recipientFlags), "recipient")
221  	warnDuplicates(slices.Values(recipientsFileFlags), "recipients file")
222  	warnDuplicates(func(yield func(string) bool) {
223  		for _, f := range identityFlags {
224  			if f.Type == "i" && !yield(f.Value) {
225  				return
226  			}
227  		}
228  	}, "identity file")
229  
230  	var inUseFiles []string
231  	for _, i := range identityFlags {
232  		if i.Type != "i" {
233  			continue
234  		}
235  		inUseFiles = append(inUseFiles, absPath(i.Value))
236  	}
237  	for _, f := range recipientsFileFlags {
238  		inUseFiles = append(inUseFiles, absPath(f))
239  	}
240  
241  	var in io.Reader = os.Stdin
242  	var out io.Writer = os.Stdout
243  	if name := flag.Arg(0); name != "" && name != "-" {
244  		inUseFiles = append(inUseFiles, absPath(name))
245  		f, err := os.Open(name)
246  		if err != nil {
247  			errorf("failed to open input file %q: %v", name, err)
248  		}
249  		defer f.Close()
250  		in = f
251  	} else {
252  		stdinInUse = true
253  		if decryptFlag && term.IsTerminal(os.Stdin) {
254  			// If the input comes from a TTY, assume it's armored, and buffer up
255  			// to the END line (or EOF/EOT) so that a password prompt or the
256  			// output don't get in the way of typing the input. See Issue 364.
257  			buf, err := bufferTerminalInput(in)
258  			if err != nil {
259  				errorf("failed to buffer terminal input: %v", err)
260  			}
261  			in = buf
262  		}
263  	}
264  	if name := outFlag; name != "" && name != "-" {
265  		for _, f := range inUseFiles {
266  			if f == absPath(name) {
267  				errorf("input and output file are the same: %q", name)
268  			}
269  		}
270  		f := newLazyOpener(name)
271  		defer func() {
272  			if err := f.Close(); err != nil {
273  				errorf("failed to close output file %q: %v", name, err)
274  			}
275  		}()
276  		out = f
277  	} else if term.IsTerminal(os.Stdout) {
278  		buf := &bytes.Buffer{}
279  		defer func() {
280  			if out == buf {
281  				io.Copy(os.Stdout, buf)
282  			}
283  		}()
284  		if name != "-" {
285  			if decryptFlag {
286  				// Buffer the output to check it's printable.
287  				out = buf
288  				defer func() {
289  					if bytes.ContainsFunc(buf.Bytes(), func(r rune) bool {
290  						return r != '\n' && r != '\r' && r != '\t' && unicode.IsControl(r)
291  					}) {
292  						errorWithHint("refusing to output binary to the terminal",
293  							`force anyway with "-o -"`)
294  					}
295  				}()
296  			} else if !armorFlag {
297  				// If the output wouldn't be armored, refuse to send binary to
298  				// the terminal unless explicitly requested with "-o -".
299  				errorWithHint("refusing to output binary to the terminal",
300  					"did you mean to use -a/--armor?",
301  					`force anyway with "-o -"`)
302  			}
303  		}
304  		if in == os.Stdin && term.IsTerminal(os.Stdin) {
305  			// If the input comes from a TTY and output will go to a TTY,
306  			// buffer it up so it doesn't get in the way of typing the input.
307  			out = buf
308  		}
309  	}
310  
311  	switch {
312  	case decryptFlag && len(identityFlags) == 0:
313  		decryptPass(in, out)
314  	case decryptFlag:
315  		decryptNotPass(identityFlags, in, out)
316  	case passFlag:
317  		encryptPass(in, out, armorFlag)
318  	default:
319  		encryptNotPass(recipientFlags, recipientsFileFlags, identityFlags, in, out, armorFlag)
320  	}
321  }

// gh-cli / gh-cli/pkg/cmd/extension/manager.go:113-133
113  	var externalCmd *exec.Cmd
114  
115  	if ext.IsBinary() || runtime.GOOS != "windows" {
116  		externalCmd = m.newCommand(exe, forwardArgs...)
117  	} else if runtime.GOOS == "windows" {
118  		// Dispatch all extension calls through the `sh` interpreter to support executable files with a
119  		// shebang line on Windows.
120  		shExe, err := m.findSh()
121  		if err != nil {
122  			if errors.Is(err, exec.ErrNotFound) {
123  				return true, errors.New("the `sh.exe` interpreter is required. Please install Git for Windows and try again")
124  			}
125  			return true, err
126  		}
127  		forwardArgs = append([]string{"-c", `command "$@"`, "--", exe}, forwardArgs...)
128  		externalCmd = m.newCommand(shExe, forwardArgs...)
129  	}
130  	externalCmd.Stdin = stdin
131  	externalCmd.Stdout = stdout
132  	externalCmd.Stderr = stderr
133  	return true, externalCmd.Run()

// rclone / rclone/backend/local/local.go:63
63  func init() {
64  	fsi := &fs.RegInfo{
65  		Name:        "local",
66  		Description: "Local Disk",
67  		NewFs:       NewFs,
68  		CommandHelp: commandHelp,
69  		MetadataInfo: &fs.MetadataInfo{
70  			System: systemMetadataInfo,
71  			Help: `Depending on which OS is in use the local backend may return only some
72  of the system metadata. Setting system metadata is supported on all
73  OSes but setting user metadata is only supported on linux, freebsd,
74  netbsd, macOS and Solaris. It is **not** supported on Windows yet
75  ([see pkg/attrs#47](https://github.com/pkg/xattr/issues/47)).
76  
77  User metadata is stored as extended attributes (which may not be
78  supported by all file systems) under the "user.*" prefix.
79  
80  Metadata is supported on files and directories.
81  `,
82  		},
83  		Options: []fs.Option{

// go-task / go-task/taskfile/reader.go:249-405
249  func (r *Reader) Read(ctx context.Context, node Node) (*ast.TaskfileGraph, error) {
250  	// Clean up git cache after reading all taskfiles
251  	defer func() {
252  		_ = CleanGitCache()
253  	}()
254  
255  	if err := r.include(ctx, node); err != nil {
256  		return nil, err
257  	}
258  
259  	return r.graph, nil
260  }
261  
262  func (r *Reader) debugf(format string, a ...any) {
263  	if r.debugFunc != nil {
264  		r.debugFunc(fmt.Sprintf(format, a...))
265  	}
266  }
267  
268  func (r *Reader) promptf(format string, a ...any) error {
269  	if r.promptFunc != nil {
270  		return r.promptFunc(fmt.Sprintf(format, a...))
271  	}
272  	return nil
273  }
274  
275  // isTrusted checks if a URI's host matches any of the trusted hosts patterns.
276  func (r *Reader) isTrusted(uri string) bool {
277  	if len(r.trustedHosts) == 0 {
278  		return false
279  	}
280  
281  	// Parse the URI to extract the host
282  	parsedURL, err := url.Parse(uri)
283  	if err != nil {
284  		return false
285  	}
286  	host := parsedURL.Host
287  
288  	// Check against each trusted pattern (exact match including port if provided)
289  	return slices.Contains(r.trustedHosts, host)
290  }
291  
292  func (r *Reader) include(ctx context.Context, node Node) error {
293  	// Create a new vertex for the Taskfile
294  	vertex := &ast.TaskfileVertex{
295  		URI:      node.Location(),
296  		Taskfile: nil,
297  	}
298  
299  	// Add the included Taskfile to the DAG
300  	// If the vertex already exists, we return early since its Taskfile has
301  	// already been read and its children explored
302  	if err := r.graph.AddVertex(vertex); err == graph.ErrVertexAlreadyExists {
303  		return nil
304  	} else if err != nil {
305  		return err
306  	}
307  
308  	// Read and parse the Taskfile from the file and add it to the vertex
309  	var err error
310  	vertex.Taskfile, err = r.readNode(ctx, node)
311  	if err != nil {
312  		return err
313  	}
314  
315  	// Create an error group to wait for all included Taskfiles to be read
316  	var g errgroup.Group
317  
318  	// Loop over each included taskfile
319  	for _, include := range vertex.Taskfile.Includes.All() {
320  		vars := env.GetEnviron()
321  		vars.Merge(vertex.Taskfile.Vars, nil)
322  		// Start a goroutine to process each included Taskfile
323  		g.Go(func() error {
324  			cache := &templater.Cache{Vars: vars}
325  			include = &ast.Include{
326  				Namespace:      include.Namespace,
327  				Taskfile:       templater.Replace(include.Taskfile, cache),
328  				Dir:            templater.Replace(include.Dir, cache),
329  				Optional:       include.Optional,
330  				Internal:       include.Internal,
331  				Flatten:        include.Flatten,
332  				Aliases:        include.Aliases,
333  				AdvancedImport: include.AdvancedImport,
334  				Excludes:       include.Excludes,
335  				Vars:           include.Vars,
336  				Checksum:       include.Checksum,
337  			}
338  			if err := cache.Err(); err != nil {
339  				return err
340  			}
341  
342  			entrypoint, err := node.ResolveEntrypoint(include.Taskfile)
343  			if err != nil {
344  				return err
345  			}
346  
347  			include.Dir, err = node.ResolveDir(include.Dir)
348  			if err != nil {
349  				return err
350  			}
351  
352  			includeNode, err := NewNode(entrypoint, include.Dir, r.insecure,
353  				WithParent(node),
354  				WithChecksum(include.Checksum),
355  				WithCACert(r.caCert),
356  				WithCert(r.cert),
357  				WithCertKey(r.certKey),
358  			)
359  			if err != nil {
360  				if include.Optional {
361  					return nil
362  				}
363  				return err
364  			}
365  
366  			// Recurse into the included Taskfile
367  			if err := r.include(ctx, includeNode); err != nil {
368  				return err
369  			}
370  
371  			// Create an edge between the Taskfiles
372  			r.graph.Lock()
373  			defer r.graph.Unlock()
374  			edge, err := r.graph.Edge(node.Location(), includeNode.Location())
375  			if err == graph.ErrEdgeNotFound {
376  				// If the edge doesn't exist, create it
377  				err = r.graph.AddEdge(
378  					node.Location(),
379  					includeNode.Location(),
380  					graph.EdgeData([]*ast.Include{include}),
381  					graph.EdgeWeight(1),
382  				)
383  			} else {
384  				// If the edge already exists
385  				edgeData := append(edge.Properties.Data.([]*ast.Include), include)
386  				err = r.graph.UpdateEdge(
387  					node.Location(),
388  					includeNode.Location(),
389  					graph.EdgeData(edgeData),
390  					graph.EdgeWeight(len(edgeData)),
391  				)
392  			}
393  			if errors.Is(err, graph.ErrEdgeCreatesCycle) {
394  				return errors.TaskfileCycleError{
395  					Source:      node.Location(),
396  					Destination: includeNode.Location(),
397  				}
398  			}
399  			return err
400  		})
401  	}
402  
403  	// Wait for all the go routines to finish
404  	return g.Wait()
405  }

50  // Get a Call from a path or nil
51  func (r *Registry) Get(path string) *Call {
52  	r.mu.RLock()
53  	defer r.mu.RUnlock()
54  	return r.call[path]
55  }
56  
57  // List of all calls in alphabetical order
58  func (r *Registry) List() (out []*Call) {
59  	r.mu.RLock()
60  	defer r.mu.RUnlock()
61  	var keys []string
62  	for key := range r.call {
63  		keys = append(keys, key)
64  	}
65  	sort.Strings(keys)
66  	for _, key := range keys {
67  		out = append(out, r.call[key])

// rclone / rclone/fs/rc/registry.go:41-48
41  func (r *Registry) Add(call Call) {
42  	r.mu.Lock()
43  	defer r.mu.Unlock()
44  	call.Path = strings.Trim(call.Path, "/")
45  	call.Help = strings.TrimSpace(call.Help)
46  	// fs.Debugf(nil, "Adding path %q to remote control registry", call.Path) // disabled to make initialization less verbose
47  	r.call[call.Path] = &call
48  }

33

39  
40  	// SourceURL is the URL where this plugin can be found

46  	// RuntimeConfig contains the runtime-specific configuration

// go-task / go-task/setup.go:282-316
282  func (e *Executor) doVersionChecks() error {
283  	if !e.EnableVersionCheck {
284  		return nil
285  	}
286  	// Copy the version to avoid modifying the original
287  	schemaVersion := &semver.Version{}
288  	*schemaVersion = *e.Taskfile.Version
289  
290  	// Error if the Taskfile uses a schema version below v3
291  	if schemaVersion.LessThan(ast.V3) {
292  		return &errors.TaskfileVersionCheckError{
293  			URI:           e.Taskfile.Location,
294  			SchemaVersion: schemaVersion,
295  			Message:       `no longer supported. Please use v3 or above`,
296  		}
297  	}
298  
299  	// Get the current version of Task
300  	// If we can't parse the version (e.g. when its "devel"), then ignore the current version checks
301  	currentVersion, err := semver.NewVersion(version.GetVersion())
302  	if err != nil {
303  		return nil
304  	}
305  
306  	// Error if the Taskfile uses a schema version above the current version of Task
307  	if schemaVersion.GreaterThan(currentVersion) {
308  		return &errors.TaskfileVersionCheckError{
309  			URI:           e.Taskfile.Location,
310  			SchemaVersion: schemaVersion,
311  			Message:       fmt.Sprintf(`is greater than the current version of Task (%s)`, currentVersion.String()),
312  		}
313  	}
314  
315  	return nil
316  }

// rclone / rclone/fs/fs.go:825-839


// rclone / rclone/fs/features.go:294-370
294  func (ft *Features) Fill(ctx context.Context, f Fs) *Features {
295  	if do, ok := f.(Purger); ok {
296  		ft.Purge = do.Purge
297  	}
298  	if do, ok := f.(Copier); ok {
299  		ft.Copy = do.Copy
300  	}
301  	if do, ok := f.(Mover); ok {
302  		ft.Move = do.Move
303  	}
304  	if do, ok := f.(DirMover); ok {
305  		ft.DirMove = do.DirMove
306  	}
307  	if do, ok := f.(MkdirMetadataer); ok {
308  		ft.MkdirMetadata = do.MkdirMetadata
309  	}
310  	if do, ok := f.(ChangeNotifier); ok {
311  		ft.ChangeNotify = do.ChangeNotify
312  	}
313  	if do, ok := f.(UnWrapper); ok {
314  		ft.UnWrap = do.UnWrap
315  	}
316  	if do, ok := f.(Wrapper); ok {
317  		ft.WrapFs = do.WrapFs
318  		ft.SetWrapper = do.SetWrapper
319  		ft.Overlay = true // if implement UnWrap then must be an Overlay
320  	}
321  	if do, ok := f.(DirCacheFlusher); ok {
322  		ft.DirCacheFlush = do.DirCacheFlush
323  	}
324  	if do, ok := f.(PublicLinker); ok {
325  		ft.PublicLink = do.PublicLink
326  	}
327  	if do, ok := f.(PutUncheckeder); ok {
328  		ft.PutUnchecked = do.PutUnchecked
329  	}
330  	if do, ok := f.(PutStreamer); ok {
331  		ft.PutStream = do.PutStream
332  	}
333  	if do, ok := f.(MergeDirser); ok {
334  		ft.MergeDirs = do.MergeDirs
335  	}
336  	if do, ok := f.(DirSetModTimer); ok {
337  		ft.DirSetModTime = do.DirSetModTime
338  	}
339  	if do, ok := f.(CleanUpper); ok {
340  		ft.CleanUp = do.CleanUp
341  	}
342  	if do, ok := f.(ListRer); ok {
343  		ft.ListR = do.ListR
344  	}
345  	if do, ok := f.(ListPer); ok {
346  		ft.ListP = do.ListP
347  	}
348  	if do, ok := f.(Abouter); ok {
349  		ft.About = do.About
350  	}
351  	if do, ok := f.(OpenWriterAter); ok {
352  		ft.OpenWriterAt = do.OpenWriterAt
353  	}
354  	if do, ok := f.(OpenChunkWriter); ok {
355  		ft.OpenChunkWriter = do.OpenChunkWriter
356  	}
357  	if do, ok := f.(UserInfoer); ok {
358  		ft.UserInfo = do.UserInfo
359  	}
360  	if do, ok := f.(Disconnecter); ok {
361  		ft.Disconnect = do.Disconnect
362  	}
363  	if do, ok := f.(Commander); ok {
364  		ft.Command = do.Command
365  	}
366  	if do, ok := f.(Shutdowner); ok {
367  		ft.Shutdown = do.Shutdown
368  	}
369  	return ft.DisableList(GetConfig(ctx).DisableFeatures)
370  }

// rclone / rclone/fs/features.go:505-811
505  // Purger is an optional interfaces for Fs
506  type Purger interface {
507  	// Purge all files in the directory specified
508  	//
509  	// Implement this if you have a way of deleting all the files
510  	// quicker than just running Remove() on the result of List()
511  	//
512  	// Return an error if it doesn't exist
513  	Purge(ctx context.Context, dir string) error
514  }
515  
516  // Copier is an optional interface for Fs
517  type Copier interface {
518  	// Copy src to this remote using server-side copy operations.
519  	//
520  	// This is stored with the remote path given
521  	//
522  	// It returns the destination Object and a possible error
523  	//
524  	// Will only be called if src.Fs().Name() == f.Name()
525  	//
526  	// If it isn't possible then return fs.ErrorCantCopy
527  	Copy(ctx context.Context, src Object, remote string) (Object, error)
528  }
529  
530  // Mover is an optional interface for Fs
531  type Mover interface {
532  	// Move src to this remote using server-side move operations.
533  	//
534  	// This is stored with the remote path given
535  	//
536  	// It returns the destination Object and a possible error
537  	//
538  	// Will only be called if src.Fs().Name() == f.Name()
539  	//
540  	// If it isn't possible then return fs.ErrorCantMove
541  	Move(ctx context.Context, src Object, remote string) (Object, error)
542  }
543  
544  // DirMover is an optional interface for Fs
545  type DirMover interface {
546  	// DirMove moves src, srcRemote to this remote at dstRemote
547  	// using server-side move operations.
548  	//
549  	// Will only be called if src.Fs().Name() == f.Name()
550  	//
551  	// If it isn't possible then return fs.ErrorCantDirMove
552  	//
553  	// If destination exists then return fs.ErrorDirExists
554  	DirMove(ctx context.Context, src Fs, srcRemote, dstRemote string) error
555  }
556  
557  // MkdirMetadataer is an optional interface for Fs
558  type MkdirMetadataer interface {
559  	// MkdirMetadata makes the directory passed in as dir.
560  	//
561  	// It shouldn't return an error if it already exists.
562  	//
563  	// If the metadata is not nil it is set.
564  	//
565  	// It returns the directory that was created.
566  	MkdirMetadata(ctx context.Context, dir string, metadata Metadata) (Directory, error)
567  }
568  
569  // ChangeNotifier is an optional interface for Fs
570  type ChangeNotifier interface {
571  	// ChangeNotify calls the passed function with a path
572  	// that has had changes. If the implementation
573  	// uses polling, it should adhere to the given interval.
574  	// At least one value will be written to the channel,
575  	// specifying the initial value and updated values might
576  	// follow. A 0 Duration should pause the polling.
577  	// The ChangeNotify implementation must empty the channel
578  	// regularly. When the channel gets closed, the implementation
579  	// should stop polling and release resources.
580  	ChangeNotify(context.Context, func(string, EntryType), <-chan time.Duration)
581  }
582  
583  // EntryType can be associated with remote paths to identify their type
584  type EntryType int
585  
586  // Constants
587  const (
588  	// EntryDirectory should be used to classify remote paths in directories
589  	EntryDirectory EntryType = iota // 0
590  	// EntryObject should be used to classify remote paths in objects
591  	EntryObject // 1
592  )
593

598  }

619  	// May create the object even if it returns an error - if so
620  	// will return the object and the error, otherwise will return
621  	// nil and the error
622  	//
623  	// May create duplicates or return errors if src already
624  	// exists.
625  	PutUnchecked(ctx context.Context, in io.Reader, src ObjectInfo, options ...OpenOption) (Object, error)
626  }

634  	// nil and the error

666  // ListRer is an optional interfaces for Fs
667  type ListRer interface {
668  	// ListR lists the objects and directories of the Fs starting
669  	// from dir recursively into out.
670  	//
671  	// dir should be "" to start from the root, and should not
672  	// have trailing slashes.
673  	//
674  	// This should return ErrDirNotFound if the directory isn't
675  	// found.
676  	//
677  	// It should call callback for each tranche of entries read.
678  	// These need not be returned in any particular order.  If
679  	// callback returns an error then the listing will stop
680  	// immediately.
681  	//
682  	// Don't implement this unless you have a more efficient way
683  	// of listing recursively that doing a directory traversal.
684  	ListR(ctx context.Context, dir string, callback ListRCallback) error
685  }
686  
687  // ListPer is an optional interfaces for Fs
688  type ListPer interface {
689  	// ListP lists the objects and directories of the Fs starting
690  	// from dir non recursively into out.
691  	//
692  	// dir should be "" to start from the root, and should not
693  	// have trailing slashes.
694  	//
695  	// This should return ErrDirNotFound if the directory isn't
696  	// found.
697  	//
698  	// It should call callback for each tranche of entries read.
699  	// These need not be returned in any particular order.  If
700  	// callback returns an error then the listing will stop
701  	// immediately.
702  	ListP(ctx context.Context, dir string, callback ListRCallback) error
703  }

705  // RangeSeeker is the interface that wraps the RangeSeek method.
706  //
707  // Some of the returns from Object.Open() may optionally implement
708  // this method for efficiency purposes.
709  type RangeSeeker interface {
710  	// RangeSeek behaves like a call to Seek(offset int64, whence
711  	// int) with the output wrapped in an io.LimitedReader
712  	// limiting the total length to limit.
713  	//
714  	// RangeSeek with a limit of < 0 is equivalent to a regular Seek.
715  	RangeSeek(ctx context.Context, offset int64, whence int, length int64) (int64, error)
716  }

729  	//

737  // ChunkWriterInfo describes how a backend would like ChunkWriter called
738  type ChunkWriterInfo struct {
739  	ChunkSize         int64 // preferred chunk size
740  	Concurrency       int   // how many chunks to write at once
741  	LeavePartsOnError bool  // if set don't delete parts uploaded so far on error
742  }
743  
744  // OpenChunkWriter is an option interface for Fs to implement chunked writing
745  type OpenChunkWriter interface {
746  	// OpenChunkWriter returns the chunk size and a ChunkWriter
747  	//
748  	// Pass in the remote and the src object
749  	// You can also use options to hint at the desired chunk size
750  	OpenChunkWriter(ctx context.Context, remote string, src ObjectInfo, options ...OpenOption) (info ChunkWriterInfo, writer ChunkWriter, err error)
751  }
752  
753  // OpenChunkWriterFn describes the OpenChunkWriter function pointer
754  type OpenChunkWriterFn func(ctx context.Context, remote string, src ObjectInfo, options ...OpenOption) (info ChunkWriterInfo, writer ChunkWriter, err error)
755  
756  // ChunkWriter is returned by OpenChunkWriter to implement chunked writing
757  type ChunkWriter interface {
758  	// WriteChunk will write chunk number with reader bytes, where chunk number >= 0
759  	WriteChunk(ctx context.Context, chunkNumber int, reader io.ReadSeeker) (bytesWritten int64, err error)
760  
761  	// Close complete chunked writer finalising the file.
762  	Close(ctx context.Context) error
763  
764  	// Abort chunk write
765  	//
766  	// You can and should call Abort without calling Close.
767  	Abort(ctx context.Context) error
768  }

774  }

780  }

782  // CommandHelp describes a single backend Command
783  //
784  // These are automatically inserted in the docs
785  type CommandHelp struct {

787  	Short string            // Single line description
788  	Long  string            // Long multi-line description
789  	Opts  map[string]string // maps option name to a single line help
790  }

792  // Commander is an interface to wrap the Command function
793  type Commander interface {
794  	// Command the backend to run a named command
795  	//
796  	// The command run is name
797  	// args may be used to read arguments from
798  	// opts may be used to read optional arguments from
799  	//
800  	// The result should be capable of being JSON encoded
801  	// If it is a string or a []string it will be shown to the user
802  	// otherwise it will be JSON encoded and shown to the user like that
803  	Command(ctx context.Context, name string, arg []string, opt map[string]string) (any, error)
804  }

807  type Shutdowner interface {

811  }

87  		`)

// go-task / go-task/internal/templater/templater.go:65-112
65  func ReplaceWithExtra[T any](v T, cache *Cache, extra map[string]any) T {
66  	// If there is already an error, do nothing
67  	if cache.err != nil {
68  		return v
69  	}
70  
71  	// Optimization: skip if string is not a template
72  	if s, ok := any(v).(string); ok {
73  		if !strings.Contains(s, "{{") {
74  			return v
75  		}
76  	}
77  
78  	// Initialize the cache map if it's not already initialized
79  	if cache.cacheMap == nil {
80  		cache.cacheMap = cache.Vars.ToCacheMap()
81  	}
82  
83  	// Create a copy of the cache map to avoid editing the original
84  	// If there is extra data, merge it with the cache map
85  	data := maps.Clone(cache.cacheMap)
86  	if extra != nil {
87  		maps.Copy(data, extra)
88  	}
89  
90  	// Traverse the value and parse any template variables
91  	copy, err := deepcopy.TraverseStringsFunc(v, func(v string) (string, error) {
92  		// Optimization: skip if string is not a template
93  		if !strings.Contains(v, "{{") {
94  			return v, nil
95  		}
96  		tpl, err := template.New("").Funcs(templateFuncs).Parse(v)
97  		if err != nil {
98  			return v, err
99  		}
100  		var b bytes.Buffer
101  		if err := tpl.Execute(&b, data); err != nil {
102  			return v, err
103  		}
104  		return strings.ReplaceAll(b.String(), "<no value>", ""), nil
105  	})
106  	if err != nil {
107  		cache.err = err
108  		return v
109  	}
110  
111  	return copy
112  }

// fzf / fzf/src/server.go:40-145
40  type httpServer struct {
41  	apiKey        []byte
42  	actionChannel chan []*action
43  	getHandler    func(getParams) string
44  }
45  
46  type listenAddress struct {
47  	host string
48  	port int
49  	sock string
50  }
51  
52  func (addr listenAddress) IsLocal() bool {
53  	return addr.host == "localhost" || addr.host == "127.0.0.1" || len(addr.sock) > 0
54  }
55  
56  var defaultListenAddr = listenAddress{"localhost", 0, ""}
57  
58  func parseListenAddress(address string) (listenAddress, error) {
59  	if strings.HasSuffix(address, ".sock") {
60  		return listenAddress{"", 0, address}, nil
61  	}
62  
63  	parts := strings.SplitN(address, ":", 3)
64  	if len(parts) == 1 {
65  		parts = []string{"localhost", parts[0]}
66  	}
67  	if len(parts) != 2 {
68  		return defaultListenAddr, fmt.Errorf("invalid listen address: %s", address)
69  	}
70  	portStr := parts[len(parts)-1]
71  	port, err := strconv.Atoi(portStr)
72  	if err != nil || port < 0 || port > 65535 {
73  		return defaultListenAddr, fmt.Errorf("invalid listen port: %s", portStr)
74  	}
75  	if len(parts[0]) == 0 {
76  		parts[0] = "localhost"
77  	}
78  	return listenAddress{parts[0], port, ""}, nil
79  }
80  
81  func startHttpServer(address listenAddress, actionChannel chan []*action, getHandler func(getParams) string) (net.Listener, int, error) {
82  	host := address.host
83  	port := address.port
84  	apiKey := os.Getenv("FZF_API_KEY")
85  	if !address.IsLocal() && len(apiKey) == 0 {
86  		return nil, port, errors.New("FZF_API_KEY is required to allow remote access")
87  	}
88  
89  	var listener net.Listener
90  	var err error
91  	if len(address.sock) > 0 {
92  		if _, err := os.Stat(address.sock); err == nil {
93  			// Check if the socket is already in use
94  			if conn, err := net.Dial("unix", address.sock); err == nil {
95  				conn.Close()
96  				return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("socket already in use: %s", address.sock)
97  			}
98  			os.Remove(address.sock)
99  		}
100  		listener, err = net.Listen("unix", address.sock)
101  		if err != nil {
102  			return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to listen on %s", address.sock)
103  		}
104  		os.Chmod(address.sock, 0600)
105  	} else {
106  		addrStr := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", host, port)
107  		listener, err = net.Listen("tcp", addrStr)
108  		if err != nil {
109  			return nil, port, fmt.Errorf("failed to listen on %s", addrStr)
110  		}
111  		if port == 0 {
112  			addr := listener.Addr().String()
113  			parts := strings.Split(addr, ":")
114  			if len(parts) < 2 {
115  				return nil, port, fmt.Errorf("cannot extract port: %s", addr)
116  			}
117  			var err error
118  			port, err = strconv.Atoi(parts[len(parts)-1])
119  			if err != nil {
120  				return nil, port, err
121  			}
122  		}
123  	}
124  
125  	go func() {
126  		server := httpServer{
127  			apiKey:        []byte(apiKey),
128  			actionChannel: actionChannel,
129  			getHandler:    getHandler,
130  		}
131  		for {
132  			conn, err := listener.Accept()
133  			if err != nil {
134  				if errors.Is(err, net.ErrClosed) {
135  					return
136  				}
137  				continue
138  			}
139  			conn.Write([]byte(server.handleHttpRequest(conn)))
140  			conn.Close()
141  		}
142  	}()
143  
144  	return listener, port, nil
145  }

// helm / helm/internal/plugin/plugin.go:27-50
27  type Plugin interface {
28  	// Dir return the plugin directory (as an absolute path) on the filesystem
29  	Dir() string
30  
31  	// Metadata describes the plugin's type, version, etc.
32  	// (This metadata type is the converted and plugin version independented in-memory representation of the plugin.yaml file)
33  	Metadata() Metadata
34  
35  	// Invoke takes the given input, and dispatches the contents to plugin instance
36  	// The input is expected to be a JSON-serializable object, which the plugin will interpret according to its type
37  	// The plugin is expected to return a JSON-serializable object, which the invoker
38  	// will interpret according to the plugin's type
39  	//
40  	// Invoke can be thought of as a request/response mechanism. Similar to e.g. http.RoundTripper
41  	//
42  	// If plugin's execution fails with a non-zero "return code" (this is plugin runtime implementation specific)
43  	// an InvokeExecError is returned
44  	Invoke(ctx context.Context, input *Input) (*Output, error)
45  }

49  	InvokeHook(event string) error
50  }

// rclone / rclone/lib/plugin/plugin.go:13-39
13  func init() {
14  	dir := os.Getenv("RCLONE_PLUGIN_PATH")
15  	if dir == "" {
16  		return
17  	}
18  	// Get file names of plugin dir
19  	listing, err := os.ReadDir(dir)
20  	if err != nil {
21  		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "Failed to open plugin directory:", err)
22  	}
23  	// Enumerate file names, load valid plugins
24  	for _, file := range listing {
25  		// Match name
26  		fileName := file.Name()
27  		if !strings.HasPrefix(fileName, "librcloneplugin_") {
28  			continue
29  		}
30  		if !strings.HasSuffix(fileName, ".so") {
31  			continue
32  		}
33  		// Try to load plugin
34  		_, err := plugin.Open(filepath.Join(dir, fileName))
35  		if err != nil {
36  			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Failed to load plugin %s: %s\n",
37  				fileName, err)
38  		}
39  	}

// restic / restic/internal/feature/features.go:60-113
60  func (f *FlagSet) Apply(flags string, logWarning func(string)) error {
61  	if flags == "" {
62  		return nil
63  	}
64  
65  	selection := make(map[string]bool)
66  
67  	for _, flag := range strings.Split(flags, ",") {
68  		parts := strings.SplitN(flag, "=", 2)
69  
70  		name := parts[0]
71  		value := "true"
72  		if len(parts) == 2 {
73  			value = parts[1]
74  		}
75  
76  		isEnabled, err := strconv.ParseBool(value)
77  		if err != nil {
78  			return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse value %q for feature flag %v: %w", value, name, err)
79  		}
80  
81  		selection[name] = isEnabled
82  	}
83  
84  	for name, value := range selection {
85  		fname := FlagName(name)
86  		flag := f.flags[fname]
87  		if flag == nil {
88  			return fmt.Errorf("unknown feature flag %q", name)
89  		}
90  
91  		switch flag.Type {
92  		case Alpha, Beta:
93  			f.enabled[fname] = value
94  		case Stable:
95  			logWarning(fmt.Sprintf("feature flag %q is always enabled and will be removed in a future release", fname))
96  		case Deprecated:
97  			logWarning(fmt.Sprintf("feature flag %q is always disabled and will be removed in a future release", fname))
98  		default:
99  			panic("unknown feature phase")
100  		}
101  	}
102  
103  	return nil
104  }
105  
106  func (f *FlagSet) Enabled(name FlagName) bool {
107  	isEnabled, ok := f.enabled[name]
108  	if !ok {
109  		panic(fmt.Sprintf("unknown feature flag %v", name))
110  	}
111  
112  	return isEnabled
113  }

--- age (8/10) ---
File: /home/antonioborgerees/coding/ultraplan/studies/go-cli-study/reports/source/12-extensibility/age.md

# Repo Analysis: age

## Extensibility & Plugin Design

### Repo Info

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Name | age |
| Path | `/home/antonioborgerees/coding/go-cli-study/repos/age` |
| Group | `go-cli-study` |
| Language / Stack | Go |
| Analyzed | 2026-05-15 |

## Summary

age is a file encryption tool that implements a well-designed plugin system for extensibility. Plugins are external binaries invoked via a stanza-based protocol over stdin/stdout. The core provides `Recipient` and `Identity` interfaces that plugins can implement. The `plugin.Plugin` framework simplifies plugin development by handling all protocol details. Extension points are clearly defined and versioned (v1).

## Rating

**8/10** — Well-designed modularity. The plugin system is a first-class feature with a clear protocol, but third-party extension beyond plugins requires modifying core source due to internal package boundaries.

## Evidence Collected

| Area | Evidence | File:Line |
|------|----------|-----------|
| Plugin interface | `Recipient` and `Identity` interfaces in core package | `age.go:65-86` |
| Plugin loader | Client spawns `age-plugin-{name}` binary | `plugin/client.go:426-433` |
| Plugin framework | `Plugin` struct with `HandleRecipient`, `HandleIdentity` callbacks | `plugin/plugin.go:29-118` |
| Plugin protocol | Stanza-based v1 protocol with `recipient-v1` and `identity-v1` states | `plugin/plugin.go:159-167` |
| Command registration | CLI uses standard `flag` package, no dynamic registration | `cmd/age/age.go:122-140` |
| Internal packages | `internal/format`, `internal/stream`, `internal/bech32` | `internal/format/format.go:1`, `internal/stream/stream.go:1` |
| Version stability | Backwards compatibility policy documented | `age.go:37-46` |
| Plugin examples | `extra/age-plugin-pq`, `extra/age-plugin-tag` | `extra/age-plugin-pq/plugin-pq.go:1` |
| UI callbacks | `ClientUI` struct with `DisplayMessage`, `RequestValue`, `Confirm` | `plugin/client.go:314-337` |
| RecipientWithLabels | Optional interface for label-based recipient grouping | `age.go:88-101` |

## Answers to Protocol Questions

### 1. Can new commands be added cleanly?

No. The CLI (`cmd/age/age.go`) is a single monolithic program with hardcoded command handling in `main()` (`cmd/age/age.go:105-321`). There is no command registry or plugin-based command extension. Adding new commands requires modifying the core source.

### 2. Is extension anticipated?

Yes, but only for recipients/identities via the plugin system. The `Recipient` (`age.go:82-86`) and `Identity` (`age.go:65-73`) interfaces are the primary extension points. The plugin protocol is documented and versioned. However, there is no mechanism to extend CLI subcommands.

### 3. Are interfaces stable?

Yes. The plugin protocol uses explicit versioning (`recipient-v1`, `identity-v1` at `plugin/plugin.go:159-164`). The `Plugin.Main()` dispatch is based on `--age-plugin` flag value. Core interfaces like `Recipient` and `Identity` are well-defined and backed by a backwards compatibility policy (`age.go:37-46`).

### 4. Are internal APIs modular?

Partially. Internal packages (`internal/format`, `internal/stream`, `internal/bech32`) are clearly separated. However, they are not exported and plugins cannot access them directly—plugins interact only through the established plugin protocol. The `internal` directory convention prevents external imports (`internal/format/format.go:6`).

## Architectural Decisions

- **Plugin as external process**: Plugins are separate binaries invoked via `exec.Command`, communicating through a stanza-based protocol on stdin/stdout (`plugin/client.go:426-473`). This provides process isolation and language neutrality.
- **Plugin discovery by naming convention**: Plugin binaries must be named `age-plugin-{name}` and found in `$PATH` (`plugin/client.go:427`). No plugin registry or configuration.
- **UI callbacks for interactivity**: Plugins can request user interaction (messages, confirmations, secrets) through structured stanzas handled by `ClientUI` (`plugin/client.go:314-337`).
- **Bech32-encoded identities**: Plugin recipient/identity strings use Bech32 encoding with a plugin-specific prefix (`AGE-PLUGIN-NAME-1...`) (`plugin/encode.go`).

## Notable Patterns

- **Interface-based recipient/identity**: Core encryption accepts any `Recipient` or `Identity` implementation (`age.go:154,249`), enabling built-in (X25519, Scrypt, hybrid) and plugin-based recipients.
- **Stanza protocol**: Both file format and plugin protocol use a line-based stanza format with base64-encoded bodies (`internal/format/format.go:108-132`, `plugin/plugin.go:192-229`).
- **Single-binary plugin framework**: The `plugin.Plugin` type handles all protocol state machines, allowing developers to expose their `Recipient`/`Identity` implementations as plugins with minimal code (`plugin/plugin.go:29-167`).

## Tradeoffs

- **Plugin scope limited to crypto operations**: Plugins can only provide new `Recipient`/`Identity` implementations, not new CLI commands or other functionality.
- **No plugin configuration**: Plugin discovery is solely by binary name in `$PATH`. There is no way to configure plugin paths, versions, or options.
- **Process spawn overhead**: Each `Wrap`/`Unwrap` call spawns a new plugin process (`plugin/client.go:465`), which has overhead for batch operations.
- **Internal packages inaccessible**: Third parties cannot build on internal packages, even for non-plugin extension scenarios.

## Failure Modes / Edge Cases

- **Plugin not found**: `NotFoundError` returned when `age-plugin-{name}` binary is absent (`plugin/client.go:466-467`). CLI provides helpful error message suggesting installation.
- **Plugin protocol mismatch**: If plugin and client disagree on protocol version, the plugin returns exit code 4 (`plugin/plugin.go:165-166`).
- **Plugin error handling**: Errors during `Wrap`/`Unwrap` are returned as stanza payloads, with exit codes 1-3 indicating different error types (`plugin/plugin.go:601-613`).
- **Interactive plugin without TTY**: If a plugin requests user interaction but the client provides no `ClientUI` implementation, the plugin receives a "fail" response (`plugin/client.go:342-348`).

## Future Considerations

- Command/plugin registry configuration file to specify plugin paths and versions.
- Plugin API versioning mechanism to allow graceful fallback between protocol versions.
- Consideration for plugin batch operations to reduce per-file spawn overhead.
- Potential v2 plugin protocol if backwards compatibility requirements constrain future changes.

## Questions / Gaps

- No evidence of a plugin signature/authentication mechanism to verify plugin authenticity.
- No evidence of plugin resource limits (memory, time) to prevent malicious or buggy plugins.
- Internal packages (`internal/inspect`, `internal/term`, `internal/stream`) are not documented as stable even within the same major version.

---

Generated by `12-extensibility.md` against `age`.

// age / age.go:37-46
37  // # Backwards compatibility
38  //
39  // Files encrypted with a stable version (not alpha, beta, or release candidate)
40  // of age, or with any v1.0.0 beta or release candidate, will decrypt with any
41  // later versions of the v1 API. This might change in v2, in which case v1 will
42  // be maintained with security fixes for compatibility with older files.
43  //
44  // If decrypting an older file poses a security risk, doing so might require an
45  // explicit opt-in in the API.
46  package age

// age / plugin/client.go:314-337
314  // ClientUI holds callbacks that will be invoked by (Un)Wrap if the plugin
315  // wishes to interact with the user. If any of them is nil or returns an error,
316  // failure will be reported to the plugin, but note that the error is otherwise
317  // discarded. Implementations are encouraged to display errors to the user
318  // before returning them.
319  type ClientUI struct {
320  	// DisplayMessage displays the message, which is expected to have lowercase
321  	// initials and no final period.
322  	DisplayMessage func(name, message string) error
323  
324  	// RequestValue requests a secret or public input, with the provided prompt.
325  	RequestValue func(name, prompt string, secret bool) (string, error)
326  
327  	// Confirm requests a confirmation with the provided prompt. The yes and no
328  	// value are the choices provided to the user. no may be empty. The return
329  	// value indicates whether the user selected the yes or no option.
330  	Confirm func(name, prompt, yes, no string) (choseYes bool, err error)
331  
332  	// WaitTimer is invoked once (Un)Wrap has been waiting for 5 seconds on the
333  	// plugin, for example because the plugin is waiting for an external event
334  	// (e.g. a hardware token touch). Unlike the other callbacks, WaitTimer runs
335  	// in a separate goroutine, and if missing it's simply ignored.
336  	WaitTimer func(name string)
337  }

// age / age.go:88-101
88  // RecipientWithLabels can be optionally implemented by a [Recipient], in which
89  // case [Encrypt] will use WrapWithLabels instead of [Recipient.Wrap].
90  //
91  // Encrypt will succeed only if the labels returned by all the recipients
92  // (assuming the empty set for those that don't implement RecipientWithLabels)
93  // are the same.
94  //
95  // This can be used to ensure a recipient is only used with other recipients
96  // with equivalent properties (for example by setting a "postquantum" label) or
97  // to ensure a recipient is always used alone (by returning a random label, for
98  // example to preserve its authentication properties).
99  type RecipientWithLabels interface {
100  	WrapWithLabels(fileKey []byte) (s []*Stanza, labels []string, err error)
101  }

// age / plugin/client.go:426-473
426  func openClientConnection(name, protocol string) (*clientConnection, error) {
427  	path := "age-plugin-" + name
428  	if testOnlyPluginPath != "" {
429  		path = filepath.Join(testOnlyPluginPath, path)
430  	} else if strings.ContainsRune(name, os.PathSeparator) {
431  		return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid plugin name: %q", name)
432  	}
433  	cmd := exec.Command(path, "--age-plugin="+protocol)
434  
435  	stdout, err := cmd.StdoutPipe()
436  	if err != nil {
437  		return nil, err
438  	}
439  	stdin, err := cmd.StdinPipe()
440  	if err != nil {
441  		return nil, err
442  	}
443  
444  	cc := &clientConnection{
445  		cmd:    cmd,
446  		Reader: stdout,
447  		Writer: stdin,
448  		close: func() {
449  			stdin.Close()
450  			stdout.Close()
451  		},
452  	}
453  
454  	if os.Getenv("AGEDEBUG") == "plugin" {
455  		cc.Reader = io.TeeReader(cc.Reader, os.Stderr)
456  		cc.Writer = io.MultiWriter(cc.Writer, os.Stderr)
457  		cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
458  	}
459  
460  	// We don't want the plugins to rely on the working directory for anything
461  	// as different clients might treat it differently, so we set it to an empty
462  	// temporary directory.
463  	cmd.Dir = os.TempDir()
464  
465  	if err := cmd.Start(); err != nil {
466  		if errors.Is(err, exec.ErrNotFound) {
467  			return nil, &NotFoundError{Name: name, Err: err}
468  		}
469  		return nil, err
470  	}
471  
472  	return cc, nil
473  }

// age / plugin/client.go:427

431  		return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid plugin name: %q", name)
432  	}
433  	cmd := exec.Command(path, "--age-plugin="+protocol)
434

437  		return nil, err
438  	}
439  	stdin, err := cmd.StdinPipe()
440  	if err != nil {
441  		return nil, err
442  	}
443

445  		cmd:    cmd,
446  		Reader: stdout,
447  		Writer: stdin,

// age / age.go:154,249
154  func Encrypt(dst io.Writer, recipients ...Recipient) (io.WriteCloser, error) {
155  	fileKey := make([]byte, fileKeySize)
156  	rand.Read(fileKey)
157  
158  	hdr, err := encryptHdr(fileKey, recipients...)
159  	if err != nil {
160  		return nil, err
161  	}
162  	if err := hdr.Marshal(dst); err != nil {
163  		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to write header: %w", err)
164  	}
165  
166  	nonce := make([]byte, streamNonceSize)
167  	rand.Read(nonce)
168  	if _, err := dst.Write(nonce); err != nil {
169  		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to write nonce: %w", err)
170  	}
171  
172  	return stream.NewEncryptWriter(streamKey(fileKey, nonce), dst)
173  }
174  
175  // EncryptReader encrypts a file to one or more recipients. Every recipient will be
176  // able to decrypt the file.
177  //
178  // Reads from the returned Reader produce the encrypted file, where the plaintext
179  // is read from src.
180  func EncryptReader(src io.Reader, recipients ...Recipient) (io.Reader, error) {
181  	fileKey := make([]byte, fileKeySize)
182  	rand.Read(fileKey)
183  
184  	hdr, err := encryptHdr(fileKey, recipients...)
185  	if err != nil {
186  		return nil, err
187  	}
188  	buf := &bytes.Buffer{}
189  	if err := hdr.Marshal(buf); err != nil {
190  		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to prepare header: %w", err)
191  	}
192  
193  	nonce := make([]byte, streamNonceSize)
194  	rand.Read(nonce)
195  
196  	r, err := stream.NewEncryptReader(streamKey(fileKey, nonce), src)
197  	if err != nil {
198  		return nil, err
199  	}
200  	return io.MultiReader(buf, bytes.NewReader(nonce), r), nil
201  }
202  
203  func wrapWithLabels(r Recipient, fileKey []byte) (s []*Stanza, labels []string, err error) {
204  	if r, ok := r.(RecipientWithLabels); ok {
205  		return r.WrapWithLabels(fileKey)
206  	}
207  	s, err = r.Wrap(fileKey)
208  	return
209  }
210  
211  func incompatibleLabelsError(l1, l2 []string) error {
212  	hasPQ1 := slices.Contains(l1, "postquantum")
213  	hasPQ2 := slices.Contains(l2, "postquantum")
214  	if hasPQ1 != hasPQ2 {
215  		return fmt.Errorf("incompatible recipients: can't mix post-quantum and classic recipients, or the file would be vulnerable to quantum computers")
216  	}
217  	return fmt.Errorf("incompatible recipients: %q and %q can't be mixed", l1, l2)
218  }
219  
220  // NoIdentityMatchError is returned by [Decrypt] when none of the supplied
221  // identities match the encrypted file.
222  type NoIdentityMatchError struct {
223  	// Errors is a slice of all the errors returned to Decrypt by the Unwrap
224  	// calls it made. They all wrap [ErrIncorrectIdentity].
225  	Errors []error
226  	// StanzaTypes are the first argument of each recipient stanza in the
227  	// encrypted file's header.
228  	StanzaTypes []string
229  }
230  
231  func (e *NoIdentityMatchError) Error() string {
232  	if len(e.Errors) == 1 {
233  		return "identity did not match any of the recipients: " + e.Errors[0].Error()
234  	}
235  	return "no identity matched any of the recipients"
236  }
237  
238  func (e *NoIdentityMatchError) Unwrap() []error {
239  	return e.Errors
240  }
241  
242  // Decrypt decrypts a file encrypted to one or more identities.
243  // All identities will be tried until one successfully decrypts the file.
244  // Native, non-interactive identities are tried before any other identities.
245  //
246  // Decrypt returns a Reader reading the decrypted plaintext of the age file read
247  // from src. If no identity matches the encrypted file, the returned error will
248  // be of type [NoIdentityMatchError].
249  func Decrypt(src io.Reader, identities ...Identity) (io.Reader, error) {

// age / internal/format/format.go:108-132
108  var stanzaPrefix = []byte("->")
109  var footerPrefix = []byte("---")
110  
111  func (r *Stanza) Marshal(w io.Writer) error {
112  	if _, err := w.Write(stanzaPrefix); err != nil {
113  		return err
114  	}
115  	for _, a := range append([]string{r.Type}, r.Args...) {
116  		if _, err := io.WriteString(w, " "+a); err != nil {
117  			return err
118  		}
119  	}
120  	if _, err := io.WriteString(w, "\n"); err != nil {
121  		return err
122  	}
123  	ww := NewWrappedBase64Encoder(b64, w)
124  	if _, err := ww.Write(r.Body); err != nil {
125  		return err
126  	}
127  	if err := ww.Close(); err != nil {
128  		return err
129  	}
130  	_, err := io.WriteString(w, "\n")
131  	return err
132  }

// age / plugin/plugin.go:192-229
192  	p.sr = format.NewStanzaReader(bufio.NewReader(p.stdin))
193  ReadLoop:
194  	for {
195  		s, err := p.sr.ReadStanza()
196  		if err != nil {
197  			return p.fatalf("failed to read stanza: %v", err)
198  		}
199  
200  		switch s.Type {
201  		case "add-recipient":
202  			if err := expectStanzaWithNoBody(s, 1); err != nil {
203  				return p.fatalf("%v", err)
204  			}
205  			recipientStrings = append(recipientStrings, s.Args[0])
206  		case "add-identity":
207  			if err := expectStanzaWithNoBody(s, 1); err != nil {
208  				return p.fatalf("%v", err)
209  			}
210  			identityStrings = append(identityStrings, s.Args[0])
211  		case "extension-labels":
212  			if err := expectStanzaWithNoBody(s, 0); err != nil {
213  				return p.fatalf("%v", err)
214  			}
215  			supportsLabels = true
216  		case "wrap-file-key":
217  			if err := expectStanzaWithBody(s, 0); err != nil {
218  				return p.fatalf("%v", err)
219  			}
220  			fileKeys = append(fileKeys, s.Body)
221  		case "done":
222  			if err := expectStanzaWithNoBody(s, 0); err != nil {
223  				return p.fatalf("%v", err)
224  			}
225  			break ReadLoop
226  		default:
227  			// Unsupported stanzas in uni-directional phases are ignored.
228  		}
229  	}

// age / plugin/plugin.go:29-167
29  type Plugin struct {
30  	name string
31  	fs   *flag.FlagSet
32  	sm   *string
33  
34  	recipient     func([]byte) (age.Recipient, error)
35  	idAsRecipient func([]byte) (age.Recipient, error)
36  	identity      func([]byte) (age.Identity, error)
37  
38  	stdin          io.Reader
39  	stdout, stderr io.Writer
40  
41  	sr *format.StanzaReader
42  	// broken is set if the protocol broke down during an interaction function
43  	// called by a Recipient or Identity.
44  	broken bool
45  }
46  
47  // New creates a new Plugin with the given name.
48  //
49  // For example, a plugin named "frood" would be invoked as "age-plugin-frood".
50  func New(name string) (*Plugin, error) {
51  	return &Plugin{name: name, stdin: os.Stdin,
52  		stdout: os.Stdout, stderr: os.Stderr}, nil
53  }
54  
55  // Name returns the name of the plugin.
56  func (p *Plugin) Name() string {
57  	return p.name
58  }
59  
60  // RegisterFlags registers the plugin's flags with the given [flag.FlagSet], or
61  // with the default [flag.CommandLine] if fs is nil. It must be called before
62  // [flag.Parse] and [Plugin.Main].
63  //
64  // This allows the plugin to expose additional flags when invoked manually, for
65  // example to implement a keygen mode.
66  func (p *Plugin) RegisterFlags(fs *flag.FlagSet) {
67  	if fs == nil {
68  		fs = flag.CommandLine
69  	}
70  	p.fs = fs
71  	p.sm = fs.String("age-plugin", "", "age-plugin state machine")
72  }
73  
74  // HandleRecipient registers a function to parse recipients of the form
75  // age1name1... into [age.Recipient] values. data is the decoded Bech32 payload.
76  //
77  // If the returned Recipient implements [age.RecipientWithLabels], Plugin will
78  // use it and enforce consistency across every returned stanza in an execution.
79  // If the client supports labels, they will be passed through the protocol.
80  //
81  // It must be called before [Plugin.Main], and can be called at most once.
82  // Otherwise, it panics.
83  func (p *Plugin) HandleRecipient(f func(data []byte) (age.Recipient, error)) {
84  	if p.recipient != nil {
85  		panic("HandleRecipient called twice")
86  	}
87  	p.recipient = f
88  }
89  
90  // HandleIdentityAsRecipient registers a function to parse identities of the
91  // form AGE-PLUGIN-NAME-1... into [age.Recipient] values, for when identities
92  // are used as recipients. data is the decoded Bech32 payload.
93  //
94  // If the returned Recipient implements [age.RecipientWithLabels], Plugin will
95  // use it and enforce consistency across every returned stanza in an execution.
96  // If the client supports labels, they will be passed through the protocol.
97  //
98  // It must be called before [Plugin.Main], and can be called at most once.
99  // Otherwise, it panics.
100  func (p *Plugin) HandleIdentityAsRecipient(f func(data []byte) (age.Recipient, error)) {
101  	if p.idAsRecipient != nil {
102  		panic("HandleIdentityAsRecipient called twice")
103  	}
104  	p.idAsRecipient = f
105  }
106  
107  // HandleIdentity registers a function to parse identities of the form
108  // AGE-PLUGIN-NAME-1... into [age.Identity] values. data is the decoded Bech32
109  // payload.
110  //
111  // It must be called before [Plugin.Main], and can be called at most once.
112  // Otherwise, it panics.
113  func (p *Plugin) HandleIdentity(f func(data []byte) (age.Identity, error)) {
114  	if p.identity != nil {
115  		panic("HandleIdentity called twice")
116  	}
117  	p.identity = f
118  }
119  
120  // HandleRecipientEncoding is like [Plugin.HandleRecipient] but provides the
121  // full recipient encoding string to the callback.
122  //
123  // It allows using functions like ParseRecipient directly.
124  func (p *Plugin) HandleRecipientEncoding(f func(recipient string) (age.Recipient, error)) {
125  	p.HandleRecipient(func(data []byte) (age.Recipient, error) {
126  		return f(EncodeRecipient(p.name, data))
127  	})
128  }
129  
130  // HandleIdentityEncodingAsRecipient is like [Plugin.HandleIdentityAsRecipient] but
131  // provides the full identity encoding string to the callback.
132  func (p *Plugin) HandleIdentityEncodingAsRecipient(f func(identity string) (age.Recipient, error)) {
133  	p.HandleIdentityAsRecipient(func(data []byte) (age.Recipient, error) {
134  		return f(EncodeIdentity(p.name, data))
135  	})
136  }
137  
138  // HandleIdentityEncoding is like [Plugin.HandleIdentity] but provides the
139  // full identity encoding string to the callback.
140  //
141  // It allows using functions like ParseIdentity directly.
142  func (p *Plugin) HandleIdentityEncoding(f func(identity string) (age.Identity, error)) {
143  	p.HandleIdentity(func(data []byte) (age.Identity, error) {
144  		return f(EncodeIdentity(p.name, data))
145  	})
146  }
147  
148  // Main runs the plugin protocol. It returns an exit code to pass to os.Exit.
149  //
150  // It automatically calls [Plugin.RegisterFlags] and [flag.Parse] if they were
151  // not called before.
152  func (p *Plugin) Main() int {
153  	if p.fs == nil {
154  		p.RegisterFlags(nil)
155  	}
156  	if !p.fs.Parsed() {
157  		p.fs.Parse(os.Args[1:])
158  	}
159  	if *p.sm == "recipient-v1" {
160  		return p.RecipientV1()
161  	}
162  	if *p.sm == "identity-v1" {
163  		return p.IdentityV1()
164  	}
165  	fmt.Fprintf(p.stderr, "unknown state machine %q", *p.sm)
166  	return 4
167  }

// age / plugin/client.go:465

466  		if errors.Is(err, exec.ErrNotFound) {
467  			return nil, &NotFoundError{Name: name, Err: err}
468  		}
469  		return nil, err
470  	}
471  
472  	return cc, nil
473  }
474  
475  func (cc *clientConnection) Close() error {
476  	// Close stdin and stdout and send SIGINT (if supported) to the plugin,
477  	// then wait for it to cleanup and exit.
478  	cc.close()
479  	cc.cmd.Process.Signal(os.Interrupt)
480  	return cc.cmd.Wait()
481  }

483  func writeStanza(conn io.Writer, t string, args ...string) error {
484  	s := &format.Stanza{Type: t, Args: args}
485  	return s.Marshal(conn)
