43  		//  - RespondQueryTaskCompletedRequest
44  		// If waitLocalActivity is true, and there is outstanding local activities, this call will return nil.
45  		CompleteWorkflowTask(workflowTask *workflowTask, waitLocalActivity bool) workflowTaskCompletion
46  		// GetWorkflowTaskTimeout returns the WorkflowTaskTimeout
47  		GetWorkflowTaskTimeout() time.Duration
48  		GetCurrentWorkflowTask() *workflowservice.PollWorkflowTaskQueueResponse
49  		IsDestroyed() bool
50  		StackTrace() string
51  	}
52  
53  	EventLevelResetter func(int64)
54  
55  	// WorkflowTaskHandler represents workflow task handlers.
56  	WorkflowTaskHandler interface {
57  		WorkflowContextManager
58  
59  		// Processes the workflow task
60  		// The response could be:
61  		//  - RespondWorkflowTaskCompletedRequest
62  		//  - RespondWorkflowTaskFailedRequest
63  		//  - RespondQueryTaskCompletedRequest
64  		ProcessWorkflowTask(
65  			task *workflowTask,
66  			ctx *workflowExecutionContextImpl,
67  			f workflowTaskHeartbeatFunc,
68  		) (taskCompletion *workflowTaskCompletion, err error)

// sdk-go / internal/internal_update.go:79-200
79  	// updateProtocol wraps an updateEnv and some protocol metadata to
80  	// implement the UpdateCallbacks abstraction. It handles callbacks by
81  	// sending protocol messages.
82  	updateProtocol struct {
83  		protoInstanceID  string
84  		clientIdentity   string
85  		initialRequest   *updatepb.Request
86  		requestMsgID     string
87  		requestSeqID     int64
88  		scheduleUpdate   func(name string, id string, args *commonpb.Payloads, header *commonpb.Header, callbacks UpdateCallbacks)
89  		env              updateEnv
90  		state            updateState
91  		dataConverter    converter.DataConverter
92  		failureConverter converter.FailureConverter
93  	}
94  
95  	// updateHandler is the underlying type that is registered into a workflow
96  	// environment when the user-code in a workflow registers an update callback
97  	// for a given name. It offers the ability to invoke the associated
98  	// execution and validation functions.
99  	updateHandler struct {
100  		fn               interface{}
101  		validateFn       interface{}
102  		name             string
103  		unfinishedPolicy HandlerUnfinishedPolicy
104  		description      string
105  		dataConverter    converter.DataConverter
106  		failureConverter converter.FailureConverter
107  	}
108  )
109  
110  // newUpdateResponder constructs an updateProtocolResponder instance to handle
111  // update callbacks.
112  func newUpdateProtocol(
113  	protoInstanceID string,
114  	scheduleUpdate func(name string, id string, args *commonpb.Payloads, header *commonpb.Header, callbacks UpdateCallbacks),
115  	env updateEnv,
116  ) *updateProtocol {
117  	return &updateProtocol{
118  		protoInstanceID:  protoInstanceID,
119  		env:              env,
120  		scheduleUpdate:   scheduleUpdate,
121  		state:            updateStateNew,
122  		dataConverter:    env.GetDataConverter(),
123  		failureConverter: env.GetFailureConverter(),
124  	}
125  }
126  
127  func (up *updateProtocol) requireState(action string, valid ...updateState) {
128  	for _, validState := range valid {
129  		if up.state == validState {
130  			return
131  		}
132  	}
133  	panicIllegalState(fmt.Sprintf("[TMPRL1100] invalid action %q in update protocol %+v", action, up))
134  }
135  
136  func (up *updateProtocol) HandleMessage(msg *protocolpb.Message) error {
137  	var request updatepb.Request
138  	if err := msg.Body.UnmarshalTo(&request); err != nil {
139  		return err
140  	}
141  	up.initialRequest = &request
142  	up.requireState("update request", updateStateNew)
143  	up.requestMsgID = msg.GetId()
144  	up.requestSeqID = msg.GetEventId()
145  	input := up.initialRequest.GetInput()
146  	up.scheduleUpdate(input.GetName(), up.initialRequest.GetMeta().GetUpdateId(), input.GetArgs(), input.GetHeader(), up)
147  	up.state = updateStateRequestInitiated
148  	return nil
149  }
150  
151  // Accept is called for an update after it has passed validation and
152  // before execution has started.
153  func (up *updateProtocol) Accept() {
154  	up.requireState("accept", updateStateRequestInitiated)
155  	up.env.Send(&protocolpb.Message{
156  		Id:                 up.protoInstanceID + "/accept",
157  		ProtocolInstanceId: up.protoInstanceID,
158  		Body: protocol.MustMarshalAny(&updatepb.Acceptance{
159  			AcceptedRequestMessageId:         up.requestMsgID,
160  			AcceptedRequestSequencingEventId: up.requestSeqID,
161  			AcceptedRequest:                  up.initialRequest,
162  		}),
163  	}, withExpectedEventPredicate(up.checkAcceptedEvent))
164  	// Stop holding a reference to the initial request to allow it to be GCed
165  	up.initialRequest = nil
166  	up.state = updateStateAccepted
167  }
168  
169  // Reject is called for an update if validation fails.
170  func (up *updateProtocol) Reject(err error) {
171  	up.requireState("reject", updateStateNew, updateStateRequestInitiated)
172  	up.env.Send(&protocolpb.Message{
173  		Id:                 up.protoInstanceID + "/reject",
174  		ProtocolInstanceId: up.protoInstanceID,
175  		Body: protocol.MustMarshalAny(&updatepb.Rejection{
176  			RejectedRequestMessageId:         up.requestMsgID,
177  			RejectedRequestSequencingEventId: up.requestSeqID,
178  			RejectedRequest:                  up.initialRequest,
179  			Failure:                          up.failureConverter.ErrorToFailure(err),
180  		}),
181  	})
182  	up.state = updateStateCompleted
183  }
184  
185  // Complete is called for an update with the result of executing the
186  // update function.
187  func (up *updateProtocol) Complete(success interface{}, outcomeErr error) {
188  	up.requireState("complete", updateStateAccepted)
189  	outcome := &updatepb.Outcome{}
190  	if outcomeErr != nil {
191  		outcome.Value = &updatepb.Outcome_Failure{
192  			Failure: up.failureConverter.ErrorToFailure(outcomeErr),
193  		}
194  	} else {
195  		dc := up.dataConverter
196  		success, err := dc.ToPayloads(success)
197  		if err != nil {
198  			panic(err)
199  		}
200  		outcome.Value = &updatepb.Outcome_Success{

// t3code / packages/contracts/src/orchestration.ts:1278
1278  ) {}
1279  

// t3code / apps/server/src/orchestration/projector.ts:167-653
167  export function projectEvent(
168    model: OrchestrationReadModel,
169    event: OrchestrationEvent,
170  ): Effect.Effect<OrchestrationReadModel, OrchestrationProjectorDecodeError> {
171    const nextBase: OrchestrationReadModel = {
172      ...model,
173      snapshotSequence: event.sequence,
174      updatedAt: event.occurredAt,
175    };
176  
177    switch (event.type) {
178      case "project.created":
179        return decodeForEvent(ProjectCreatedPayload, event.payload, event.type, "payload").pipe(
180          Effect.map((payload) => {
181            const existing = nextBase.projects.find((entry) => entry.id === payload.projectId);
182            const nextProject = {
183              id: payload.projectId,
184              title: payload.title,
185              workspaceRoot: payload.workspaceRoot,
186              defaultModelSelection: payload.defaultModelSelection,
187              scripts: payload.scripts,
188              createdAt: payload.createdAt,
189              updatedAt: payload.updatedAt,
190              deletedAt: null,
191            };
192  
193            return {
194              ...nextBase,
195              projects: existing
196                ? nextBase.projects.map((entry) =>
197                    entry.id === payload.projectId ? nextProject : entry,
198                  )
199                : [...nextBase.projects, nextProject],
200            };
201          }),
202        );
203  
204      case "project.meta-updated":
205        return decodeForEvent(ProjectMetaUpdatedPayload, event.payload, event.type, "payload").pipe(
206          Effect.map((payload) => ({
207            ...nextBase,
208            projects: nextBase.projects.map((project) =>
209              project.id === payload.projectId
210                ? {
211                    ...project,
212                    ...(payload.title !== undefined ? { title: payload.title } : {}),
213                    ...(payload.workspaceRoot !== undefined
214                      ? { workspaceRoot: payload.workspaceRoot }
215                      : {}),
216                    ...(payload.defaultModelSelection !== undefined
217                      ? { defaultModelSelection: payload.defaultModelSelection }
218                      : {}),
219                    ...(payload.scripts !== undefined ? { scripts: payload.scripts } : {}),
220                    updatedAt: payload.updatedAt,
221                  }
222                : project,
223            ),
224          })),
225        );
226  
227      case "project.deleted":
228        return decodeForEvent(ProjectDeletedPayload, event.payload, event.type, "payload").pipe(
229          Effect.map((payload) => ({
230            ...nextBase,
231            projects: nextBase.projects.map((project) =>
232              project.id === payload.projectId
233                ? {
234                    ...project,
235                    deletedAt: payload.deletedAt,
236                    updatedAt: payload.deletedAt,
237                  }
238                : project,
239            ),
240          })),
241        );
242  
243      case "thread.created":
244        return Effect.gen(function* () {
245          const payload = yield* decodeForEvent(
246            ThreadCreatedPayload,
247            event.payload,
248            event.type,
249            "payload",
250          );
251          const thread: OrchestrationThread = yield* decodeForEvent(
252            OrchestrationThread,
253            {
254              id: payload.threadId,
255              projectId: payload.projectId,
256              title: payload.title,
257              modelSelection: payload.modelSelection,
258              runtimeMode: payload.runtimeMode,
259              interactionMode: payload.interactionMode,
260              branch: payload.branch,
261              worktreePath: payload.worktreePath,
262              latestTurn: null,
263              createdAt: payload.createdAt,
264              updatedAt: payload.updatedAt,
265              archivedAt: null,
266              deletedAt: null,
267              messages: [],
268              activities: [],
269              checkpoints: [],
270              session: null,
271            },
272            event.type,
273            "thread",
274          );
275          const existing = nextBase.threads.find((entry) => entry.id === thread.id);
276          return {
277            ...nextBase,
278            threads: existing
279              ? nextBase.threads.map((entry) => (entry.id === thread.id ? thread : entry))
280              : [...nextBase.threads, thread],
281          };
282        });
283  
284      case "thread.deleted":
285        return decodeForEvent(ThreadDeletedPayload, event.payload, event.type, "payload").pipe(
286          Effect.map((payload) => ({
287            ...nextBase,
288            threads: updateThread(nextBase.threads, payload.threadId, {
289              deletedAt: payload.deletedAt,
290              updatedAt: payload.deletedAt,
291            }),
292          })),
293        );
294  
295      case "thread.archived":
296        return decodeForEvent(ThreadArchivedPayload, event.payload, event.type, "payload").pipe(
297          Effect.map((payload) => ({
298            ...nextBase,
299            threads: updateThread(nextBase.threads, payload.threadId, {
300              archivedAt: payload.archivedAt,
301              updatedAt: payload.updatedAt,
302            }),
303          })),
304        );
305  
306      case "thread.unarchived":
307        return decodeForEvent(ThreadUnarchivedPayload, event.payload, event.type, "payload").pipe(
308          Effect.map((payload) => ({
309            ...nextBase,
310            threads: updateThread(nextBase.threads, payload.threadId, {
311              archivedAt: null,
312              updatedAt: payload.updatedAt,
313            }),
314          })),
315        );
316  
317      case "thread.meta-updated":
318        return decodeForEvent(ThreadMetaUpdatedPayload, event.payload, event.type, "payload").pipe(
319          Effect.map((payload) => ({
320            ...nextBase,
321            threads: updateThread(nextBase.threads, payload.threadId, {
322              ...(payload.title !== undefined ? { title: payload.title } : {}),
323              ...(payload.modelSelection !== undefined
324                ? { modelSelection: payload.modelSelection }
325                : {}),
326              ...(payload.branch !== undefined ? { branch: payload.branch } : {}),
327              ...(payload.worktreePath !== undefined ? { worktreePath: payload.worktreePath } : {}),
328              updatedAt: payload.updatedAt,
329            }),
330          })),
331        );
332  
333      case "thread.runtime-mode-set":
334        return decodeForEvent(ThreadRuntimeModeSetPayload, event.payload, event.type, "payload").pipe(
335          Effect.map((payload) => ({
336            ...nextBase,
337            threads: updateThread(nextBase.threads, payload.threadId, {
338              runtimeMode: payload.runtimeMode,
339              updatedAt: payload.updatedAt,
340            }),
341          })),
342        );
343  
344      case "thread.interaction-mode-set":
345        return decodeForEvent(
346          ThreadInteractionModeSetPayload,
347          event.payload,
348          event.type,
349          "payload",
350        ).pipe(
351          Effect.map((payload) => ({
352            ...nextBase,
353            threads: updateThread(nextBase.threads, payload.threadId, {
354              interactionMode: payload.interactionMode,
355              updatedAt: payload.updatedAt,
356            }),
357          })),
358        );
359  
360      case "thread.message-sent":
361        return Effect.gen(function* () {
362          const payload = yield* decodeForEvent(
363            MessageSentPayloadSchema,
364            event.payload,
365            event.type,
366            "payload",
367          );
368          const thread = nextBase.threads.find((entry) => entry.id === payload.threadId);
369          if (!thread) {
370            return nextBase;
371          }
372  
373          const message: OrchestrationMessage = yield* decodeForEvent(
374            OrchestrationMessage,
375            {
376              id: payload.messageId,
377              role: payload.role,
378              text: payload.text,
379              ...(payload.attachments !== undefined ? { attachments: payload.attachments } : {}),
380              turnId: payload.turnId,
381              streaming: payload.streaming,
382              createdAt: payload.createdAt,
383              updatedAt: payload.updatedAt,
384            },
385            event.type,
386            "message",
387          );
388  
389          const existingMessage = thread.messages.find((entry) => entry.id === message.id);
390          const messages = existingMessage
391            ? thread.messages.map((entry) =>
392                entry.id === message.id
393                  ? {
394                      ...entry,
395                      text: message.streaming
396                        ? `${entry.text}${message.text}`
397                        : message.text.length > 0
398                          ? message.text
399                          : entry.text,
400                      streaming: message.streaming,
401                      updatedAt: message.updatedAt,
402                      turnId: message.turnId,
403                      ...(message.attachments !== undefined
404                        ? { attachments: message.attachments }
405                        : {}),
406                    }
407                  : entry,
408              )
409            : [...thread.messages, message];
410          const cappedMessages = messages.slice(-MAX_THREAD_MESSAGES);
411  
412          return {
413            ...nextBase,
414            threads: updateThread(nextBase.threads, payload.threadId, {
415              messages: cappedMessages,
416              updatedAt: event.occurredAt,
417            }),
418          };
419        });
420  
421      case "thread.session-set":
422        return Effect.gen(function* () {
423          const payload = yield* decodeForEvent(
424            ThreadSessionSetPayload,
425            event.payload,
426            event.type,
427            "payload",
428          );
429          const thread = nextBase.threads.find((entry) => entry.id === payload.threadId);
430          if (!thread) {
431            return nextBase;
432          }
433  
434          const session: OrchestrationSession = yield* decodeForEvent(
435            OrchestrationSession,
436            payload.session,
437            event.type,
438            "session",
439          );
440  
441          return {
442            ...nextBase,
443            threads: updateThread(nextBase.threads, payload.threadId, {
444              session,
445              latestTurn:
446                session.status === "running" && session.activeTurnId !== null
447                  ? {
448                      turnId: session.activeTurnId,
449                      state: "running",
450                      requestedAt:
451                        thread.latestTurn?.turnId === session.activeTurnId
452                          ? thread.latestTurn.requestedAt
453                          : session.updatedAt,
454                      startedAt:
455                        thread.latestTurn?.turnId === session.activeTurnId
456                          ? (thread.latestTurn.startedAt ?? session.updatedAt)
457                          : session.updatedAt,
458                      completedAt: null,
459                      assistantMessageId:
460                        thread.latestTurn?.turnId === session.activeTurnId
461                          ? thread.latestTurn.assistantMessageId
462                          : null,
463                    }
464                  : thread.latestTurn,
465              updatedAt: event.occurredAt,
466            }),
467          };
468        });
469  
470      case "thread.proposed-plan-upserted":
471        return Effect.gen(function* () {
472          const payload = yield* decodeForEvent(
473            ThreadProposedPlanUpsertedPayload,
474            event.payload,
475            event.type,
476            "payload",
477          );
478          const thread = nextBase.threads.find((entry) => entry.id === payload.threadId);
479          if (!thread) {
480            return nextBase;
481          }
482  
483          const proposedPlans = [
484            ...thread.proposedPlans.filter((entry) => entry.id !== payload.proposedPlan.id),
485            payload.proposedPlan,
486          ]
487            .toSorted(
488              (left, right) =>
489                left.createdAt.localeCompare(right.createdAt) || left.id.localeCompare(right.id),
490            )
491            .slice(-200);
492  
493          return {
494            ...nextBase,
495            threads: updateThread(nextBase.threads, payload.threadId, {
496              proposedPlans,
497              updatedAt: event.occurredAt,
498            }),
499          };
500        });
501  
502      case "thread.turn-diff-completed":
503        return Effect.gen(function* () {
504          const payload = yield* decodeForEvent(
505            ThreadTurnDiffCompletedPayload,
506            event.payload,
507            event.type,
508            "payload",
509          );
510          const thread = nextBase.threads.find((entry) => entry.id === payload.threadId);
511          if (!thread) {
512            return nextBase;
513          }
514  
515          const checkpoint = yield* decodeForEvent(
516            OrchestrationCheckpointSummary,
517            {
518              turnId: payload.turnId,
519              checkpointTurnCount: payload.checkpointTurnCount,
520              checkpointRef: payload.checkpointRef,
521              status: payload.status,
522              files: payload.files,
523              assistantMessageId: payload.assistantMessageId,
524              completedAt: payload.completedAt,
525            },
526            event.type,
527            "checkpoint",
528          );
529  
530          // Do not let a placeholder (status "missing") overwrite a checkpoint
531          // that has already been captured with a real git ref (status "ready").
532          // ProviderRuntimeIngestion may fire multiple turn.diff.updated events
533          // per turn; without this guard later placeholders would clobber the
534          // real capture dispatched by CheckpointReactor.
535          const existing = thread.checkpoints.find((entry) => entry.turnId === checkpoint.turnId);
536          if (existing && existing.status !== "missing" && checkpoint.status === "missing") {
537            return nextBase;
538          }
539  
540          const checkpoints = [
541            ...thread.checkpoints.filter((entry) => entry.turnId !== checkpoint.turnId),
542            checkpoint,
543          ]
544            .toSorted((left, right) => left.checkpointTurnCount - right.checkpointTurnCount)
545            .slice(-MAX_THREAD_CHECKPOINTS);
546  
547          return {
548            ...nextBase,
549            threads: updateThread(nextBase.threads, payload.threadId, {
550              checkpoints,
551              latestTurn: {
552                turnId: payload.turnId,
553                state: checkpointStatusToLatestTurnState(payload.status),
554                requestedAt:
555                  thread.latestTurn?.turnId === payload.turnId
556                    ? thread.latestTurn.requestedAt
557                    : payload.completedAt,
558                startedAt:
559                  thread.latestTurn?.turnId === payload.turnId
560                    ? (thread.latestTurn.startedAt ?? payload.completedAt)
561                    : payload.completedAt,
562                completedAt: payload.completedAt,
563                assistantMessageId: payload.assistantMessageId,
564              },
565              updatedAt: event.occurredAt,
566            }),
567          };
568        });
569  
570      case "thread.reverted":
571        return decodeForEvent(ThreadRevertedPayload, event.payload, event.type, "payload").pipe(
572          Effect.map((payload) => {
573            const thread = nextBase.threads.find((entry) => entry.id === payload.threadId);
574            if (!thread) {
575              return nextBase;
576            }
577  
578            const checkpoints = thread.checkpoints
579              .filter((entry) => entry.checkpointTurnCount <= payload.turnCount)
580              .toSorted((left, right) => left.checkpointTurnCount - right.checkpointTurnCount)
581              .slice(-MAX_THREAD_CHECKPOINTS);
582            const retainedTurnIds = new Set(checkpoints.map((checkpoint) => checkpoint.turnId));
583            const messages = retainThreadMessagesAfterRevert(
584              thread.messages,
585              retainedTurnIds,
586              payload.turnCount,
587            ).slice(-MAX_THREAD_MESSAGES);
588            const proposedPlans = retainThreadProposedPlansAfterRevert(
589              thread.proposedPlans,
590              retainedTurnIds,
591            ).slice(-200);
592            const activities = retainThreadActivitiesAfterRevert(thread.activities, retainedTurnIds);
593  
594            const latestCheckpoint = checkpoints.at(-1) ?? null;
595            const latestTurn =
596              latestCheckpoint === null
597                ? null
598                : {
599                    turnId: latestCheckpoint.turnId,
600                    state: checkpointStatusToLatestTurnState(latestCheckpoint.status),
601                    requestedAt: latestCheckpoint.completedAt,
602                    startedAt: latestCheckpoint.completedAt,
603                    completedAt: latestCheckpoint.completedAt,
604                    assistantMessageId: latestCheckpoint.assistantMessageId,
605                  };
606  
607            return {
608              ...nextBase,
609              threads: updateThread(nextBase.threads, payload.threadId, {
610                checkpoints,
611                messages,
612                proposedPlans,
613                activities,
614                latestTurn,
615                updatedAt: event.occurredAt,
616              }),
617            };
618          }),
619        );
620  
621      case "thread.activity-appended":
622        return decodeForEvent(
623          ThreadActivityAppendedPayload,
624          event.payload,
625          event.type,
626          "payload",
627        ).pipe(
628          Effect.map((payload) => {
629            const thread = nextBase.threads.find((entry) => entry.id === payload.threadId);
630            if (!thread) {
631              return nextBase;
632            }
633  
634            const activities = [
635              ...thread.activities.filter((entry) => entry.id !== payload.activity.id),
636              payload.activity,
637            ]
638              .toSorted(compareThreadActivities)
639              .slice(-500);
640  
641            return {
642              ...nextBase,
643              threads: updateThread(nextBase.threads, payload.threadId, {
644                activities,
645                updatedAt: event.occurredAt,
646              }),
647            };
648          }),
649        );
650  
651      default:
652        return Effect.succeed(nextBase);
653    }

// t3code / apps/server/src/orchestration/Layers/OrchestrationEngine.ts:88-89
88    const commandQueue = yield* Queue.unbounded<CommandEnvelope>();
89    const eventPubSub = yield* PubSub.unbounded<OrchestrationEvent>();

// go-plugin / client.go:580-948
580  func (c *Client) Start() (addr net.Addr, err error) {
581  	c.l.Lock()
582  	defer c.l.Unlock()
583  
584  	if c.address != nil {
585  		return c.address, nil
586  	}
587  
588  	// If one of cmd or reattach isn't set, then it is an error. We wrap
589  	// this in a {} for scoping reasons, and hopeful that the escape
590  	// analysis will pop the stack here.
591  	{
592  		var mutuallyExclusiveOptions int
593  		if c.config.Cmd != nil {
594  			mutuallyExclusiveOptions += 1
595  		}
596  		if c.config.Reattach != nil {
597  			mutuallyExclusiveOptions += 1
598  		}
599  		if c.config.RunnerFunc != nil {
600  			mutuallyExclusiveOptions += 1
601  		}
602  		if mutuallyExclusiveOptions != 1 {
603  			return nil, fmt.Errorf("exactly one of Cmd, or Reattach, or RunnerFunc must be set")
604  		}
605  
606  		if c.config.SecureConfig != nil && c.config.Reattach != nil {
607  			return nil, ErrSecureConfigAndReattach
608  		}
609  
610  		if c.config.GRPCBrokerMultiplex && c.config.Reattach != nil {
611  			return nil, fmt.Errorf("gRPC broker multiplexing is not supported with Reattach config")
612  		}
613  	}
614  
615  	if c.config.Reattach != nil {
616  		return c.reattach()
617  	}
618  
619  	if c.config.VersionedPlugins == nil {
620  		c.config.VersionedPlugins = make(map[int]PluginSet)
621  	}
622  
623  	// handle all plugins as versioned, using the handshake config as the default.
624  	version := int(c.config.ProtocolVersion)
625  
626  	// Make sure we're not overwriting a real version 0. If ProtocolVersion was
627  	// non-zero, then we have to just assume the user made sure that
628  	// VersionedPlugins doesn't conflict.
629  	if _, ok := c.config.VersionedPlugins[version]; !ok && c.config.Plugins != nil {
630  		c.config.VersionedPlugins[version] = c.config.Plugins
631  	}
632  
633  	var versionStrings []string
634  	for v := range c.config.VersionedPlugins {
635  		versionStrings = append(versionStrings, strconv.Itoa(v))
636  	}
637  
638  	env := []string{
639  		fmt.Sprintf("%s=%s", c.config.MagicCookieKey, c.config.MagicCookieValue),
640  		fmt.Sprintf("PLUGIN_MIN_PORT=%d", c.config.MinPort),
641  		fmt.Sprintf("PLUGIN_MAX_PORT=%d", c.config.MaxPort),
642  		fmt.Sprintf("PLUGIN_PROTOCOL_VERSIONS=%s", strings.Join(versionStrings, ",")),
643  	}
644  	if c.config.GRPCBrokerMultiplex {
645  		env = append(env, fmt.Sprintf("%s=true", envMultiplexGRPC))
646  	}
647  
648  	cmd := c.config.Cmd
649  	if cmd == nil {
650  		// It's only possible to get here if RunnerFunc is non-nil, but we'll
651  		// still use cmd as a spec to populate metadata for the external
652  		// implementation to consume.
653  		cmd = exec.Command("")
654  	}
655  	if !c.config.SkipHostEnv {
656  		cmd.Env = append(cmd.Env, os.Environ()...)
657  	}
658  	cmd.Env = append(cmd.Env, env...)
659  	cmd.Stdin = os.Stdin
660  
661  	if c.config.SecureConfig != nil {
662  		if ok, err := c.config.SecureConfig.Check(cmd.Path); err != nil {
663  			return nil, fmt.Errorf("error verifying checksum: %s", err)
664  		} else if !ok {
665  			return nil, ErrChecksumsDoNotMatch
666  		}
667  	}
668  
669  	// Setup a temporary certificate for client/server mtls, and send the public
670  	// certificate to the plugin.
671  	if c.config.AutoMTLS {
672  		c.logger.Debug("configuring client automatic mTLS")
673  		certPEM, keyPEM, err := generateCert()
674  		if err != nil {
675  			c.logger.Error("failed to generate client certificate", "error", err)
676  			return nil, err
677  		}
678  		cert, err := tls.X509KeyPair(certPEM, keyPEM)
679  		if err != nil {
680  			c.logger.Error("failed to parse client certificate", "error", err)
681  			return nil, err
682  		}
683  
684  		cmd.Env = append(cmd.Env, fmt.Sprintf("PLUGIN_CLIENT_CERT=%s", certPEM))
685  
686  		c.config.TLSConfig = &tls.Config{
687  			Certificates: []tls.Certificate{cert},
688  			ClientAuth:   tls.RequireAndVerifyClientCert,
689  			MinVersion:   tls.VersionTLS12,
690  			ServerName:   "localhost",
691  		}
692  	}
693  
694  	if c.config.UnixSocketConfig != nil {
695  		c.unixSocketCfg = *c.config.UnixSocketConfig
696  	}
697  
698  	if c.unixSocketCfg.Group != "" {
699  		cmd.Env = append(cmd.Env, fmt.Sprintf("%s=%s", EnvUnixSocketGroup, c.unixSocketCfg.Group))
700  	}
701  
702  	var runner runner.Runner
703  	switch {
704  	case c.config.RunnerFunc != nil:
705  		c.unixSocketCfg.socketDir, err = os.MkdirTemp(c.unixSocketCfg.TempDir, "plugin-dir")
706  		if err != nil {
707  			return nil, err
708  		}
709  		// os.MkdirTemp creates folders with 0o700, so if we have a group
710  		// configured we need to make it group-writable.
711  		if c.unixSocketCfg.Group != "" {
712  			err = setGroupWritable(c.unixSocketCfg.socketDir, c.unixSocketCfg.Group, 0o770)
713  			if err != nil {
714  				return nil, err
715  			}
716  		}
717  		cmd.Env = append(cmd.Env, fmt.Sprintf("%s=%s", EnvUnixSocketDir, c.unixSocketCfg.socketDir))
718  		c.logger.Trace("created temporary directory for unix sockets", "dir", c.unixSocketCfg.socketDir)
719  
720  		runner, err = c.config.RunnerFunc(c.logger, cmd, c.unixSocketCfg.socketDir)
721  		if err != nil {
722  			return nil, err
723  		}
724  	default:
725  		runner, err = cmdrunner.NewCmdRunner(c.logger, cmd)
726  		if err != nil {
727  			return nil, err
728  		}
729  
730  	}
731  
732  	c.runner = runner
733  	startCtx, startCtxCancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), c.config.StartTimeout)
734  	defer startCtxCancel()
735  	err = runner.Start(startCtx)
736  	if err != nil {
737  		return nil, err
738  	}
739  
740  	// Make sure the command is properly cleaned up if there is an error
741  	defer func() {
742  		rErr := recover()
743  
744  		if err != nil || rErr != nil {
745  			_ = runner.Kill(context.Background())
746  		}
747  
748  		if rErr != nil {
749  			panic(rErr)
750  		}
751  	}()
752  
753  	// Create a context for when we kill
754  	c.doneCtx, c.ctxCancel = context.WithCancel(context.Background())
755  
756  	// Add two to pipesWaitGroup: one for logStderr, one for the goroutine
757  	// below that consumes Stdout.  We mustn't continue to Add once we might Wait.
758  	c.pipesWaitGroup.Add(2)
759  
760  	// Start goroutine that logs the stderr
761  	c.clientWaitGroup.Add(1)
762  	// logStderr calls c.pipesWaitGroup.Done()
763  	go c.logStderr(runner.Name(), runner.Stderr())
764  
765  	c.clientWaitGroup.Add(1)
766  	go func() {
767  		// ensure the context is cancelled when we're done
768  		defer c.ctxCancel()
769  
770  		defer c.clientWaitGroup.Done()
771  
772  		// wait to finish reading from stdout/stderr since the stdout/stderr pipe readers
773  		// will be closed by the subsequent call to cmd.Wait().
774  		c.pipesWaitGroup.Wait()
775  
776  		// Wait for the command to end.
777  		err := runner.Wait(context.Background())
778  		if err != nil {
779  			c.logger.Error("plugin process exited", "plugin", runner.Name(), "id", runner.ID(), "error", err.Error())
780  		} else {
781  			// Log and make sure to flush the logs right away
782  			c.logger.Info("plugin process exited", "plugin", runner.Name(), "id", runner.ID())
783  		}
784  
785  		_ = os.Stderr.Sync()
786  
787  		// Set that we exited, which takes a lock
788  		c.l.Lock()
789  		defer c.l.Unlock()
790  		c.exited = true
791  	}()
792  
793  	// Start a goroutine that is going to be reading the lines
794  	// out of stdout
795  	linesCh := make(chan string)
796  	c.clientWaitGroup.Add(1)
797  	go func() {
798  		defer c.clientWaitGroup.Done()
799  		defer c.pipesWaitGroup.Done()
800  		defer close(linesCh)
801  
802  		scanner := bufio.NewScanner(runner.Stdout())
803  		for scanner.Scan() {
804  			linesCh <- scanner.Text()
805  		}
806  		if scanner.Err() != nil {
807  			c.logger.Error("error encountered while scanning stdout", "error", scanner.Err())
808  		}
809  	}()
810  
811  	// Make sure after we exit we read the lines from stdout forever
812  	// so they don't block since it is a pipe.
813  	// The scanner goroutine above will close this, but track it with a wait
814  	// group for completeness.
815  	c.clientWaitGroup.Add(1)
816  	defer func() {
817  		go func() {
818  			defer c.clientWaitGroup.Done()
819  			for range linesCh {
820  			}
821  		}()
822  	}()
823  
824  	// Some channels for the next step
825  	timeout := time.After(c.config.StartTimeout)
826  
827  	// Start looking for the address
828  	c.logger.Debug("waiting for RPC address", "plugin", runner.Name())
829  	select {
830  	case <-timeout:
831  		err = errors.New("timeout while waiting for plugin to start")
832  	case <-c.doneCtx.Done():
833  		err = errors.New("plugin exited before we could connect")
834  	case line, ok := <-linesCh:
835  		// Trim the line and split by "|" in order to get the parts of
836  		// the output.
837  		line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
838  		parts := strings.Split(line, "|")
839  		if len(parts) < 4 {
840  			errText := fmt.Sprintf("Unrecognized remote plugin message: %s", line)
841  			if !ok {
842  				errText += "\n" + "Failed to read any lines from plugin's stdout"
843  			}
844  			additionalNotes := runner.Diagnose(context.Background())
845  			if additionalNotes != "" {
846  				errText += "\n" + additionalNotes
847  			}
848  			err = errors.New(errText)
849  			return
850  		}
851  
852  		// Check the core protocol. Wrapped in a {} for scoping.
853  		{
854  			var coreProtocol int
855  			coreProtocol, err = strconv.Atoi(parts[0])
856  			if err != nil {
857  				err = fmt.Errorf("error parsing core protocol version: %s", err)
858  				return
859  			}
860  
861  			if coreProtocol != CoreProtocolVersion {
862  				err = fmt.Errorf("incompatible core API version with plugin. "+
863  					"Plugin version: %s, Core version: %d\n\n"+
864  					"To fix this, the plugin usually only needs to be recompiled.\n"+
865  					"Please report this to the plugin author", parts[0], CoreProtocolVersion)
866  				return
867  			}
868  		}
869  
870  		// Test the API version
871  		version, pluginSet, err := c.checkProtoVersion(parts[1])
872  		if err != nil {
873  			return addr, err
874  		}
875  
876  		// set the Plugins value to the compatible set, so the version
877  		// doesn't need to be passed through to the ClientProtocol
878  		// implementation.
879  		c.config.Plugins = pluginSet
880  		c.negotiatedVersion = version
881  		c.logger.Debug("using plugin", "version", version)
882  
883  		network, address, err := runner.PluginToHost(parts[2], parts[3])
884  		if err != nil {
885  			return addr, err
886  		}
887  
888  		switch network {
889  		case "tcp":
890  			addr, err = net.ResolveTCPAddr("tcp", address)
891  			if err != nil {
892  				return nil, err
893  			}
894  		case "unix":
895  			addr, err = net.ResolveUnixAddr("unix", address)
896  			if err != nil {
897  				return nil, err
898  			}
899  		default:
900  			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown address type: %s", address)
901  		}
902  
903  		// If we have a server type, then record that. We default to net/rpc
904  		// for backwards compatibility.
905  		c.protocol = ProtocolNetRPC
906  		if len(parts) >= 5 {
907  			c.protocol = Protocol(parts[4])
908  		}
909  
910  		found := false
911  		for _, p := range c.config.AllowedProtocols {
912  			if p == c.protocol {
913  				found = true
914  				break
915  			}
916  		}
917  		if !found {
918  			err = fmt.Errorf("unsupported plugin protocol %q. Supported: %v",
919  				c.protocol, c.config.AllowedProtocols)
920  			return addr, err
921  		}
922  
923  		// See if we have a TLS certificate from the server.
924  		// Checking if the length is > 50 rules out catching the unused "extra"
925  		// data returned from some older implementations.
926  		if len(parts) >= 6 && len(parts[5]) > 50 {
927  			err := c.loadServerCert(parts[5])
928  			if err != nil {
929  				return nil, fmt.Errorf("error parsing server cert: %s", err)
930  			}
931  		}
932  
933  		if c.config.GRPCBrokerMultiplex && c.protocol == ProtocolGRPC {
934  			if len(parts) <= 6 {
935  				return nil, fmt.Errorf("%w; for Go plugins, you will need to update the "+
936  					"github.com/hashicorp/go-plugin dependency and recompile", ErrGRPCBrokerMuxNotSupported)
937  			}
938  			if muxSupported, err := strconv.ParseBool(parts[6]); err != nil {
939  				return nil, fmt.Errorf("error parsing %q as a boolean for gRPC broker multiplexing support", parts[6])
940  			} else if !muxSupported {
941  				return nil, ErrGRPCBrokerMuxNotSupported
942  			}
943  		}
944  	}
945  
946  	c.address = addr
947  	return
948  }

// go-plugin / log_entry.go:11-76
11  // logEntry is the JSON payload that gets sent to Stderr from the plugin to the host
12  type logEntry struct {
13  	Message   string       `json:"@message"`
14  	Level     string       `json:"@level"`
15  	Timestamp time.Time    `json:"timestamp"`
16  	KVPairs   []logEntryKV `json:"kv_pairs"`
17  }
18  
19  // logEntryKV is a key value pair within the Output payload
20  type logEntryKV struct {
21  	Key   string      `json:"key"`
22  	Value interface{} `json:"value"`
23  }
24  
25  // flattenKVPairs is used to flatten KVPair slice into []interface{}
26  // for hclog consumption.
27  func flattenKVPairs(kvs []logEntryKV) []interface{} {
28  	var result []interface{}
29  	for _, kv := range kvs {
30  		result = append(result, kv.Key)
31  		result = append(result, kv.Value)
32  	}
33  
34  	return result
35  }
36  
37  // parseJSON handles parsing JSON output
38  func parseJSON(input []byte) (*logEntry, error) {
39  	var raw map[string]interface{}
40  	entry := &logEntry{}
41  
42  	err := json.Unmarshal(input, &raw)
43  	if err != nil {
44  		return nil, err
45  	}
46  
47  	// Parse hclog-specific objects
48  	if v, ok := raw["@message"]; ok {
49  		entry.Message = v.(string)
50  		delete(raw, "@message")
51  	}
52  
53  	if v, ok := raw["@level"]; ok {
54  		entry.Level = v.(string)
55  		delete(raw, "@level")
56  	}
57  
58  	if v, ok := raw["@timestamp"]; ok {
59  		t, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000000Z07:00", v.(string))
60  		if err != nil {
61  			return nil, err
62  		}
63  		entry.Timestamp = t
64  		delete(raw, "@timestamp")
65  	}
66  
67  	// Parse dynamic KV args from the hclog payload.
68  	for k, v := range raw {
69  		entry.KVPairs = append(entry.KVPairs, logEntryKV{
70  			Key:   k,
71  			Value: v,
72  		})
73  	}
74  
75  	return entry, nil
76  }

// go-plugin / grpc_stdio.go:51-83
51  func (s *grpcStdioServer) StreamStdio(
52  	_ *empty.Empty,
53  	srv plugin.GRPCStdio_StreamStdioServer,
54  ) error {
55  	// Share the same data value between runs. Sending this over the wire
56  	// marshals it so we can reuse this.
57  	var data plugin.StdioData
58  
59  	for {
60  		// Read our data
61  		select {
62  		case data.Data = <-s.stdoutCh:
63  			data.Channel = plugin.StdioData_STDOUT
64  
65  		case data.Data = <-s.stderrCh:
66  			data.Channel = plugin.StdioData_STDERR
67  
68  		case <-srv.Context().Done():
69  			return nil
70  		}
71  
72  		// Not sure if this is possible, but if we somehow got here and
73  		// we didn't populate any data at all, then just continue.
74  		if len(data.Data) == 0 {
75  			continue
76  		}
77  
78  		// Send our data to the client.
79  		if err := srv.Send(&data); err != nil {
80  			return err
81  		}
82  	}
83  }

# Repo Analysis: sdk-go

## Workflow Composition and Observability

### Repo Info

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Name | sdk-go |
| Path | `/home/antonioborgerees/coding/opencode-wrap-study/repos/sdk-go` |
| Group | `go` |
| Language / Stack | Go |
| Analyzed | 2026-05-17 |

## Summary

The sdk-go is the official Temporal Go SDK. It provides a comprehensive workflow runtime with strong separation between workflow code and the underlying Temporal server infrastructure. The SDK implements workflow primitives via a coroutine-based dispatcher model, with structured command-based scheduling for activities, child workflows, timers, and signals. Observability is achieved through an external metrics handler interface, replay-aware logging, and a query system for workflow metadata.

## Rating

**8/10** — Clear workflow primitives with useful state and progress events. The SDK provides strong observability primitives including a metrics handler interface, structured logging, and queryable workflow metadata. The command-based execution model creates a natural audit trail. Activity and workflow execution metadata is well-captured. However, the SDK does not itself generate user-facing dashboards or reports — that responsibility is deferred to external tools like the Temporal UI.

## Evidence Collected

| Area | Evidence | File:Line |
|------|----------|-----------|
| Workflow primitives | `ExecuteActivity`, `ExecuteLocalActivity`, `ExecuteChildWorkflow` functions with context-based options | `workflow/workflow.go:259-335` |
| Coroutine dispatcher | `dispatcherImpl` with `ExecuteUntilAllBlocked`, `NewCoroutine`, `IsDone` | `internal/internal_workflow.go:163-179, 1264-1321` |
| WorkflowOptions | Contains task queue, timeouts, retry policy, search attributes, memo, parent close policy | `internal/internal_workflow.go:184-219` |
| WorkflowInfo | Contains namespace, workflowID, runID, workflow type, attempt, task queue, search attributes, memo | `internal/internal_public.go:40-68` |
| Commands helper | `commandsHelper` tracks scheduled activities, timers, child workflows, signals | `internal/internal_event_handlers.go:104-174` |
| Metrics handler interface | `Handler` interface with `Counter`, `Gauge`, `Timer` and `WithTags` | `internal/common/metrics/handler.go:12-26` |

| Replay logger | `ReplayLogger` wraps user logger and respects `isReplay` flag | `internal/log/replay_logger.go` |

| Update protocol | `updateProtocol` with Accept/Reject/Complete callbacks and state machine | `internal/internal_update.go:79-200` |
| Local activity task | `localActivityTask` with attempt, retry policy, scheduled time, expire time | `internal/internal_event_handlers.go:176-192` |

| History iterator | `historyEventIteratorImpl` pages through workflow history events | `internal/internal_workflow_client.go:193-245` |
| Context propagators | `ContextPropagator` interface for propagating context across workflow boundary | `internal/internal_workflow.go:1583` |
| Search attributes | `SearchAttributes` type with typed key support | `temporal/search_attributes.go` |
| Retry policy | `RetryPolicy` with backoff, max attempts, retryable error types | `temporal/retry_policy.go` |

## Answers to Protocol Questions

### 1. What workflow primitive is used, and how much does it know about the runtime?

The SDK uses a **coroutine-based dispatcher** (`dispatcherImpl` at `internal/internal_workflow.go:163-179`) as its core workflow primitive. Workflow code runs inside coroutines (green threads) managed by this dispatcher, which executes them deterministically until all are blocked.

The dispatcher (`ExecuteUntilAllBlocked` at lines 1264-1321) runs in a single thread, processing events from workflow history in order. Each coroutine has its own `Context` which carries deadline, cancellation, and workflow-specific values.

The workflow primitive knows:
- The `WorkflowInfo` containing namespace, workflowID, runID, workflowType, attempt, taskQueueName, searchAttributes, memo, and current task build ID (`internal/internal_public.go:40-68`)
- The `WorkflowOptions` with execution parameters (timeouts, retry policy, search attributes, memo, parent close policy) (`internal/internal_workflow.go:184-219`)
- The `WorkflowEnvironment` providing access to activity scheduling, timer creation, signal handling, query handling, and update handling

The dispatcher coordinates execution but does not know about the underlying Temporal server infrastructure beyond what is passed through the WorkflowInfo and WorkflowEnvironment interfaces.

### 2. How are steps scheduled, parallelized, retried, and summarized?

**Scheduling**: Activities, child workflows, timers, signals, and nexus operations are scheduled via the `commandsHelper` in `workflowEnvironmentImpl` (`internal/internal_event_handlers.go:104-174`). Each scheduled item is assigned a sequence number and has a `handled` flag. The workflow code receives a `Future` for each scheduled operation, allowing non-blocking coordination.

**Parallelization**: The `Selector` pattern (`selectorImpl` at `internal/internal_workflow.go:140-1556`) allows waiting on multiple channels or futures concurrently. The dispatcher processes coroutines in deterministic order, yielding when blocked and unblocking when conditions are met.

**Retry**: Activities support `RetryPolicy` with backoff calculation (`internal/internal_retry.go`). The retry state is tracked in `scheduledActivity` which records the attempt number and retry policy. Local activities retry via the `laRetryCh` channel in the workflow task processing loop (`internal/internal_task_handlers.go:1000-1016`).

**Summarization**: When a workflow task completes, the command state machine processes all commands and records their results in history. The SDK records `WorkflowTaskCompleted` events with binary checksum and SDK metadata including lang used flags. Activity and local activity completion events include attempt numbers and execution latency.

### 3. How are structured runtime events projected into user-facing progress?

The SDK does **not** directly project events into user-facing dashboards. Instead, it provides observability primitives that external tools consume:

- **Metrics**: The `metrics.Handler` interface (`internal/common/metrics/handler.go:12-26`) emits counters, gauges, and timers for workflow/activity completion, latency, task scheduling, and errors. The `replayAwareHandler` (`internal/common/metrics/handler.go:77-121`) suppresses metrics during replay to avoid double-counting.

- **Logging**: The `log.Logger` interface (`log/logger.go:5-10`) supports Debug/Info/Warn/Error with structured keyvals. A `ReplayLogger` (`internal/log/replay_logger.go`) respects the `isReplay` flag to suppress logs during replay. The SDK logs activity execution (`internal/internal_event_handlers.go:807-809`), timer creation (`internal/internal_event_handlers.go:889-891`), child workflow execution (`internal/internal_event_handlers.go:634-636`), and cancellation requests.

- **Queries**: The `__temporal_workflow_metadata` query (`internal/internal_workflow.go:601-612`) returns workflow definition metadata including query/signal/update definitions. The `__temporal_stack_trace` query returns the workflow's current stack trace. Custom queries can be registered via `SetQueryHandler` (`workflow/workflow.go:604-614`).

- **Workflow metadata**: `getWorkflowMetadata` (`internal/internal_workflow.go:1657-1706`) returns a structured `WorkflowMetadata` message containing workflow type, all registered queries/signals/updates with descriptions.

The Temporal server records all history events, and the Temporal UI consumes these to present progress to users. The SDK's role is limited to emitting structured data (metrics, logs, queryable state) that the server stores.

### 4. What metadata is captured for every run, step, provider, model, and artifact?

**Run-level metadata** (`WorkflowInfo` at `internal/internal_public.go:40-68`):
- Namespace, WorkflowID, RunID, WorkflowType.Name, Attempt, TaskQueue
- SearchAttributes (custom indexed fields), Memo (unindexed), ParentWorkflowID, RootWorkflowID
- FirstExecutionRunID, ContinuedExecutionRunID, cron string
- `WorkflowExecutionTimeout`, `WorkflowRunTimeout`, `WorkflowTaskTimeout`
- `currentTaskBuildID` (set during task processing)

**Step-level metadata**:
- For activities: `ActivityType.Name`, `ActivityID`, attempt number, retry policy, scheduled time, start-to-close timeout, heartbeat timeout, priority
- For child workflows: `WorkflowType.Name`, `WorkflowID`, parent close policy, `WaitForCancellation`, attempt
- For timers: `TimerID`, start-to-fire timeout
- For signals: signal name, target workflowID/runID
- For updates: update name, update ID, validation function result

**Provider/model**: The SDK captures `WorkerBuildID` or `WorkerDeploymentVersion` in task completion events (`internal/internal_task_handlers.go:296-299`). The `binaryChecksum` records the workflow code version.

**Artifacts**: Side effects and mutable side effects are recorded with sequence IDs and their payloads stored in history. Activity results and child workflow results are stored as payloads in history events.

### 5. How are logs and durable state organized so tools can inspect active and historical runs?

**Durable state** is stored in Temporal server's history. Each workflow execution produces a sequence of history events:
- `WorkflowExecutionStarted` with input, search attributes, memo
- `WorkflowTaskScheduled`, `WorkflowTaskStarted`, `WorkflowTaskCompleted`
- `ActivityTaskScheduled`, `ActivityTaskStarted`, `ActivityTaskCompleted` (or failed/timed out/canceled)
- `TimerStarted`, `TimerFired`
- `ChildWorkflowExecutionStarted`, `ChildWorkflowExecutionCompleted`
- `SignalReceived`
- `WorkflowExecutionCompleted` with result

The history is **append-only** and fully durable. Replay reconstructs workflow state by re-processing events in order.

**Logging** is not persisted in Temporal's durable store. Logs are emitted to the `log.Logger` interface, which the user configures. The SDK includes contextual fields:
- `tagWorkflowType`, `tagWorkflowID`, `tagRunID`, `tagAttempt` in `internal/log/default_logger.go`
- The `ReplayLogger` (`internal/log/replay_logger.go`) adds a `isReplay` flag to suppress duplicate logs during replay

**Query interface**: The `__temporal_stack_trace` query allows inspecting active coroutine stack traces. The `__temporal_workflow_metadata` query returns registered handlers. Custom queries can be registered to return arbitrary workflow state.

**Metrics**: The `metrics.Handler` interface emits time-series data to external systems (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana). The SDK does not store metrics durably itself.

### 6. What should remain in the runtime wrapper versus UltraPlan-specific orchestration?

**Runtime wrapper (sdk-go)**:
- Coroutine dispatcher and deterministic execution
- Workflow code execution (activity/child workflow/timer/signal scheduling)
- Replay logic and determinism verification
- `WorkflowInfo` and `WorkflowOptions` management
- Activity/child workflow retry handling
- Context propagation across workflow boundaries
- Update protocol handling with Accept/Reject/Complete state machine
- Session management (creation, completion, heartbeat tracking)
- Metrics emission (counters, timers, gauges)
- Logging with replay awareness
- Query handling (stack trace, metadata, custom queries)
- Local activity execution within workflow task

**UltraPlan-specific (deferred to external tooling)**:
- User-facing dashboards and run progress visualization
- Workflow execution chain visualization (ContinuedAsNew, cron, retried runs)
- Cost/token accounting (requires external integration with LLM providers)
- Report generation and synthesis combining multiple runs
- Agent-specific metadata (model, provider, temperature, prompts)
- Multi-step study/sprint plan orchestration across multiple workflows
- Alerting and notification based on workflow state

## Architectural Decisions

1. **Dispatcher-as-primitive**: The SDK uses a custom coroutine dispatcher rather than native Go goroutines. This enables deterministic replay since the dispatcher controls scheduling order and can re-run the exact same schedule sequence during replay (`internal/internal_workflow.go:1264-1321`).

2. **Command-based execution**: All external operations (activity, timer, signal, child workflow) are recorded as commands in history. This creates a complete audit trail and enables Temporal server to handle failures and retries transparently (`internal/internal_event_handlers.go:104-174`).

3. **Context-based configuration**: Workflow options are passed via Go SDK's `Context` mechanism using `WithActivityOptions`, `WithChildOptions`, etc. This allows per-invocation configuration without changing the workflow function signature (`workflow/activity_options.go:21-23`).

4. **External metrics handler**: The SDK defines a minimal `metrics.Handler` interface and does not implement internal metrics storage. This allows users to integrate with any metrics system (Prometheus, StatsD, etc.) via the contrib package (`internal/common/metrics/handler.go:12-26`).

5. **Replay-aware logging**: The `ReplayLogger` wraps user loggers and consults an `isReplay` boolean pointer to suppress logs during replay, avoiding duplicate entries (`internal/log/replay_logger.go`).

6. **Update protocol as first-class primitive**: Workflow updates are implemented as a state machine (`updateProtocol` at `internal/internal_update.go:79-108`) with formal stages (New → RequestInitiated → Accepted → Completed), allowing Temporal server to sequence and deliver update outcomes reliably.

## Notable Patterns

**Coroutine-based concurrency**: Workflow code runs in "coroutines" (green threads) managed by `dispatcherImpl`. Each coroutine has a `coroutineState` with `aboutToBlock` and `unblock` channels for cooperative multitasking. This enables deterministic replay.

**Future/Channel pattern**: All async operations return `Future` handles. Operations like `ExecuteActivity` return a `Future` that becomes ready when the activity completes. The `Selector` pattern allows waiting on multiple futures/channels.

**Deterministic map iteration**: `DeterministicKeys` and `DeterministicKeysFunc` in `workflow/workflow.go:868-878` sort map keys before iteration to ensure deterministic behavior across replays.

**Context-aware data conversion**: The `DataConverter` can implement `ContextAware` interface to receive `WorkflowSerializationContext` for namespace/workflowID-specific encoding (`workflow/context.go:15-22`).

**Versioning via GetVersion**: The `GetVersion` function (`workflow/workflow.go:559-561`) records version markers in history, enabling the SDK to handle backwards-incompatible workflow changes via replay.

## Tradeoffs

1. **No native multi-step orchestration**: The SDK provides low-level primitives (activities, child workflows, timers, signals, updates). UltraPlan must build higher-level orchestration logic on top. There is no built-in DAG orchestration or step dependency management.

2. **Activity retry vs. workflow retry separation**: Activities retry independently via `RetryPolicy`, but workflow failures trigger workflow-level retry (replaying from the beginning). This can cause confusion when an activity fails but the workflow retries the entire history.

3. **Eager activity execution**: The SDK supports eager activity execution where activities start on the same worker before being scheduled by Temporal server (`internal/internal_eager_activity.go`). This improves latency but adds complexity to the execution model.

4. **No built-in cost accounting**: The SDK does not track LLM tokens, costs, or provider information. UltraPlan must implement this via custom activity context propagation or side effects.

5. **Sticky cache tradeoff**: The sticky cache (`workflowExecutionContextImpl` at `internal/internal_task_handlers.go:100-121`) speeds up subsequent workflow tasks by caching workflow state. However, this creates a dependency on a specific worker, which can cause issues during worker restarts.

6. **Coroutine vs. goroutine**: Using coroutines instead of native goroutines enables deterministic replay but introduces a custom scheduling mechanism. Workflow code cannot use native Go concurrency patterns (channels, select, goroutines) — they must use the SDK's `workflow.Channel`, `workflow.Selector`, and `workflow.Go`.

## Failure Modes / Edge Cases

1. **Non-deterministic workflow code**: If workflow code uses native Go map iteration or time.Now(), the dispatcher may replay differently. The SDK provides `DeterministicKeys` and `GetTime` to mitigate, but user errors can cause non-determinism panics.

2. **Deadlock detection timeout**: By default, if a workflow task runs longer than 1 second without yielding, the dispatcher treats it as a potential deadlock (`internal/internal_workflow.go:1153-1168`). This can trigger false positives for long-running activities.

3. **Workflow task timeout**: If a workflow task takes longer than `WorkflowTaskTimeout`, Temporal server fails the task and may dispatch it to a different worker, losing the sticky cache benefits.

4. **Activity heartbeat during cancellation**: The SDK only delivers cancellation to an activity when it heartbeats (`internal/internal_activity.go`). If an activity does not heartbeat, cancellation may be delayed up to the heartbeat timeout or never delivered.

5. **Child workflow orphan on parent exit**: If a parent workflow exits while a child is running, the `ParentClosePolicy` determines behavior. The SDK respects this policy but the actual handling depends on Temporal server.

6. **Update handler interruption**: When a workflow exits while update handlers are running, the SDK logs a warning (TMPRL1102) and can either abandon or warn-and-abandon the handlers (`internal/internal_workflow.go:716-721`).

7. **Replay of code with changes**: If workflow code has changed since a run started, replay may fail with a "binary checksum" mismatch. The SDK records the binary checksum during `WorkflowTaskCompleted` and compares on replay.

## Future Considerations

1. **Enhanced observability primitives**: The SDK could provide richer built-in support for operation-level metadata (LLM provider, model, tokens, cost) via activity/child workflow context rather than requiring UltraPlan to implement custom solutions.

2. **Built-in step orchestration**: Adding a `workflow.Step` primitive with built-in DAG support would reduce the implementation burden for UltraPlan-style tools.

3. **Structured metadata propagation**: The current context propagators mechanism (`ContextPropagator` interface) is flexible but low-level. Higher-level support for structured metadata (study ID, experiment ID, agent ID) would help UltraPlan correlate runs.

4. **Async completion for long-running activities**: The current activity heartbeat mechanism is the only way to handle long-running activities. Adding native async completion support would improve the model for LLM interactions.

5. **Query performance**: The current query mechanism requires replay to the current state. For large histories, this could be slow. Cached query handlers or snapshot-based queries could improve performance.

## Questions / Gaps

1. **No built-in token/cost tracking**: The SDK provides no primitives for capturing LLM tokens, cost, or provider information. UltraPlan must implement this via custom activity context propagation.

2. **No structured report generation**: The SDK does not generate synthesized reports from workflow runs. UltraPlan must build this on top using history queries and custom activities.

3. **No multi-workflow orchestration primitives**: There is no built-in support for orchestrating across multiple workflows (study plans, sprint plans). UltraPlan must implement this logic itself.

4. **Limited visibility into internal state**: While `WorkflowInfo` exposes some runtime state, the internal `workflowEnvironmentImpl` state (commands helper, side effects, mutable side effects) is not queryable from outside the workflow.

5. **No built-in evidence linking**: The SDK records activity results and child workflow results as payloads, but does not provide a mechanism for linking evidence (e.g., which LLM call produced which output) across workflow runs.

---

Generated by `04-workflow-composition-and-observability.md` against `sdk-go`.

// sdk-go / internal/internal_workflow.go:1657-1706
1657  func getWorkflowMetadata(ctx Context) (*sdk.WorkflowMetadata, error) {
1658  	info := GetWorkflowInfo(ctx)
1659  	eo := getWorkflowEnvOptions(ctx)
1660  	ret := &sdk.WorkflowMetadata{
1661  		Definition: &sdk.WorkflowDefinition{
1662  			Type: info.WorkflowType.Name,
1663  			QueryDefinitions: []*sdk.WorkflowInteractionDefinition{
1664  				{
1665  					Name:        QueryTypeStackTrace,
1666  					Description: "Current stack trace",
1667  				},
1668  				{
1669  					Name:        QueryTypeOpenSessions,
1670  					Description: "Open sessions on the workflow",
1671  				},
1672  				{
1673  					Name:        QueryTypeWorkflowMetadata,
1674  					Description: "Metadata about the workflow",
1675  				},
1676  			},
1677  		},
1678  		CurrentDetails: eo.currentDetails,
1679  	}
1680  	// Queries
1681  	for k, v := range eo.queryHandlers {
1682  		ret.Definition.QueryDefinitions = append(ret.Definition.QueryDefinitions, &sdk.WorkflowInteractionDefinition{
1683  			Name:        k,
1684  			Description: v.options.Description,
1685  		})
1686  	}
1687  	// Signals
1688  	for k, v := range eo.requestedSignalChannels {
1689  		ret.Definition.SignalDefinitions = append(ret.Definition.SignalDefinitions, &sdk.WorkflowInteractionDefinition{
1690  			Name:        k,
1691  			Description: v.options.Description,
1692  		})
1693  	}
1694  	// Updates
1695  	for k, v := range eo.updateHandlers {
1696  		ret.Definition.UpdateDefinitions = append(ret.Definition.UpdateDefinitions, &sdk.WorkflowInteractionDefinition{
1697  			Name:        k,
1698  			Description: v.description,
1699  		})
1700  	}
1701  	// Sort interaction definitions
1702  	sortWorkflowInteractionDefinitions(ret.Definition.QueryDefinitions)
1703  	sortWorkflowInteractionDefinitions(ret.Definition.SignalDefinitions)
1704  	sortWorkflowInteractionDefinitions(ret.Definition.UpdateDefinitions)
1705  	return ret, nil
1706  }

147  	// unblockFunc is passed evaluated by a coroutine yield. When it returns false the yield returns to a caller.
148  	// stackDepth is the depth of stack from the last blocking call relevant to user.
149  	// Used to truncate internal stack frames from thread stack.
150  	unblockFunc func(status string, stackDepth int) (keepBlocked bool)

152  	coroutineState struct {
153  		name         string
154  		dispatcher   *dispatcherImpl  // dispatcher this context belongs to
155  		aboutToBlock chan bool        // used to notify dispatcher that coroutine that owns this context is about to block
156  		unblock      chan unblockFunc // used to notify coroutine that it should continue executing.
157  		keptBlocked  bool             // true indicates that coroutine didn't make any progress since the last yield unblocking

// sdk-go / internal/internal_workflow_client.go:193-245
193  	// historyEventIteratorImpl is the implementation of HistoryEventIterator

214  // ExecuteWorkflow starts a workflow execution and returns a WorkflowRun that will allow you to wait until this workflow
215  // reaches the end state, such as workflow finished successfully or timeout.
216  // The user can use this to start using a functor like below and get the workflow execution result, as EncodedValue
217  // Either by
218  //
219  //	ExecuteWorkflow(options, "workflowTypeName", arg1, arg2, arg3)
220  //	or
221  //	ExecuteWorkflow(options, workflowExecuteFn, arg1, arg2, arg3)
222  //
223  // The current timeout resolution implementation is in seconds and uses math.Ceil(d.Seconds()) as the duration. But is
224  // subjected to change in the future.
225  //
226  // NOTE: the context.Context should have a fairly large timeout, since workflow execution may take a while to be finished
227  func (wc *WorkflowClient) ExecuteWorkflow(ctx context.Context, options StartWorkflowOptions, workflow interface{}, args ...interface{}) (WorkflowRun, error) {
228  	if err := wc.ensureInitialized(ctx); err != nil {
229  		return nil, err
230  	}
231  
232  	// Set header before interceptor run
233  	ctx = contextWithNewHeader(ctx)
234  
235  	in, err := createStartWorkflowInput(options, workflow, args, wc.registry)
236  	if err != nil {
237  		return nil, err
238  	}
239  
240  	// Run via interceptor
241  	return wc.interceptor.ExecuteWorkflow(ctx, in)
242  }
243  
244  // GetWorkflow gets a workflow execution and returns a WorkflowRun that will allow you to wait until this workflow
245  // reaches the end state, such as workflow finished successfully or timeout.

// sdk-go / internal/internal_workflow.go:1583
1583  func getWorkflowEnvOptions(ctx Context) *WorkflowOptions {
1584  	options := ctx.Value(workflowEnvOptionsContextKey)
1585  	if options != nil {
1586  		return options.(*WorkflowOptions)
1587  	}
1588  	return nil
1589  }
1590  
1591  func setWorkflowEnvOptionsIfNotExist(ctx Context) Context {
1592  	options := getWorkflowEnvOptions(ctx)
1593  	var newOptions WorkflowOptions
1594  	if options != nil {
1595  		newOptions = *options
1596  	} else {
1597  		newOptions.signalChannels = make(map[string]Channel)
1598  		newOptions.requestedSignalChannels = make(map[string]*requestedSignalChannel)
1599  		newOptions.queryHandlers = make(map[string]*queryHandler)
1600  		newOptions.updateHandlers = make(map[string]*updateHandler)
1601  		newOptions.runningUpdatesHandles = make(map[string]UpdateInfo)
1602  	}
1603  	if newOptions.DataConverter == nil {

// sdk-go / internal/internal_workflow.go:163-179
163  	dispatcherImpl struct {

166  		selectorSequence int // used to name channels

168  		executing        bool       // currently running ExecuteUntilAllBlocked. Used to avoid recursive calls to it.
169  		mutex            sync.Mutex // used to synchronize executing
170  		closed           bool

176  		// returns true if the callback updated any coroutines state and there may be more work

// sdk-go / internal/internal_workflow.go:140-1556
140  	// Implements Selector interface
141  	selectorImpl struct {

143  		cases       []*selectCase // cases that this select is comprised from
144  		defaultFunc *func()       // default case
145  	}

147  	// unblockFunc is passed evaluated by a coroutine yield. When it returns false the yield returns to a caller.
148  	// stackDepth is the depth of stack from the last blocking call relevant to user.
149  	// Used to truncate internal stack frames from thread stack.
150  	unblockFunc func(status string, stackDepth int) (keepBlocked bool)
151  
152  	coroutineState struct {
153  		name         string
154  		dispatcher   *dispatcherImpl  // dispatcher this context belongs to
155  		aboutToBlock chan bool        // used to notify dispatcher that coroutine that owns this context is about to block
156  		unblock      chan unblockFunc // used to notify coroutine that it should continue executing.
157  		keptBlocked  bool             // true indicates that coroutine didn't make any progress since the last yield unblocking
158  		closed       atomic.Bool      // indicates that owning coroutine has finished execution
159  		blocked      atomic.Bool
160  		panicError   error // non nil if coroutine had unhandled panic
161  	}

166  		selectorSequence int // used to name channels

169  		mutex            sync.Mutex // used to synchronize executing

174  		readOnly         bool

176  		// returns true if the callback updated any coroutines state and there may be more work
177  		allBlockedCallback func() bool
178  		newEagerCoroutines []*coroutineState
179  	}

193  		// WorkflowIDConflictPolicy and OnConflictOptions are only used in test environment for
194  		// running Nexus operations as child workflow.
195  		WorkflowIDConflictPolicy enumspb.WorkflowIdConflictPolicy
196  		OnConflictOptions        *OnConflictOptions

210  		queryHandlers            map[string]*queryHandler
211  		updateHandlers           map[string]*updateHandler
212  		// runningUpdatesHandles is a map of update handlers that are currently running.
213  		runningUpdatesHandles     map[string]UpdateInfo

251  	asyncFuture interface {
252  		Future
253  		// Used by selectorImpl
254  		// If Future is ready returns its value immediately.
255  		// If not registers callback which is called when it is ready.
